1вҶ’# Harmony Backend
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Express + tRPC server for the Harmony chat application.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Architecture
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Before making changes to this backend, read the unified backend architecture document:**
     8вҶ’> [`docs/unified-backend-architecture.md`](../docs/unified-backend-architecture.md)
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’The architecture doc covers:
    11вҶ’- **Module map** вҖ” what each module (M-B1вҖ“M-B7, M-D1вҖ“M-D3) owns and its boundaries
    12вҶ’- **Class diagrams** вҖ” all services, repositories, controllers, entities, and DTOs
    13вҶ’- **Data model** вҖ” ER diagram for all shared database tables
    14вҶ’- **API surface** вҖ” tRPC procedures and public REST endpoints
    15вҶ’- **Event bus** вҖ” Redis Pub/Sub event flow between modules
    16вҶ’- **Cache strategy** вҖ” Redis key layout and TTLs
    17вҶ’- **Security model** вҖ” rate limiting, visibility guards, content filtering
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’Use the module map (В§2.2) as your entry point when navigating the codebase.
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’## Development
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’```bash
    24вҶ’npm install
    25вҶ’npm run dev       # tsx watch (hot reload)
    26вҶ’npm run build     # tsc вҶ’ dist/
    27вҶ’npm run lint      # eslint src tests
    28вҶ’npm test          # jest
    29вҶ’```
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’## Setting Up Locally
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’```bash
    34вҶ’# 1. Start all required services (Postgres + Redis)
    35вҶ’docker compose up -d
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’# 2. Copy env (only once) вҖ” then edit JWT secrets before running!
    38вҶ’cp .env.example .env
    39вҶ’# Open .env and set strong, unique values for:
    40вҶ’#   JWT_ACCESS_SECRET=<random string, 32+ chars>
    41вҶ’#   JWT_REFRESH_SECRET=<random string, 32+ chars>
    42вҶ’# The placeholder values are insecure defaults: the server will start with them,
    43вҶ’# but anyone could forge tokens. The process hard-crashes on startup only if
    44вҶ’# either JWT_ACCESS_SECRET or JWT_REFRESH_SECRET is missing entirely.
    45вҶ’
    46вҶ’# 3. Apply migrations (once, and again after any schema change)
    47вҶ’npx prisma migrate deploy
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’# 4. Run tests
    50вҶ’npm test
    51вҶ’```
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’> **Why both Postgres and Redis?**
    54вҶ’> Redis is required for visibility caching and the Pub/Sub event bus (see В§4.4 and В§6 of `docs/unified-backend-architecture.md`). Starting only Postgres will disable caching and event-driven features that depend on Redis.
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’## Environment
    57вҶ’
    58вҶ’Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in values before running locally.
    59вҶ’
    60вҶ’Key variables: `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `FRONTEND_URL`, `PORT` (default `4000`).
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’> **Security:** `JWT_ACCESS_SECRET` and `JWT_REFRESH_SECRET` in `.env.example` are insecure placeholder values. The server will start with them, but tokens can be forged. Replace them with strong, unique secrets before running the server.
    63вҶ’

1вҶ’# Unified Backend Architecture вҖ” Harmony 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’> **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 featuresвҖ”**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**вҖ”in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. 4вҶ’ 5вҶ’--- 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’## 1. Design Rationale 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’### 1.2 Key Design Choices 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’| Decision | Choice | Justification | 16вҶ’|----------|--------|---------------| 17вҶ’| **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | 18вҶ’| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | 19вҶ’| **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | 20вҶ’| **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | 21вҶ’| **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | 22вҶ’| **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional serverвҶ’client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | 23вҶ’| **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | 24вҶ’| **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | 25вҶ’| **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | 26вҶ’| **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | 27вҶ’ 28вҶ’### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split 29вҶ’ 30вҶ’``` 31вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ 32вҶ’в”Ӯ API Surface в”Ӯ 33вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө 34вҶ’в”Ӯ tRPC (Authenticated)в”Ӯ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) в”Ӯ 35вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ 36вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Channel settings в”Ӯ вҖў GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) в”Ӯ 37вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Visibility toggle в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/channels/вҖҰ (messages) в”Ӯ 38вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Audit log queries в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/servers/вҖҰ (server) в”Ӯ 39вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Admin meta-tag в”Ӯ вҖў GET /sitemap/{server}.xml в”Ӯ 40вҶ’в”Ӯ overrides в”Ӯ вҖў GET /robots.txt в”Ӯ 41вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў GET /s/{server} (server landing) в”Ӯ 42вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ 43вҶ’``` 44вҶ’ 45вҶ’**Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. 46вҶ’ 47вҶ’--- 48вҶ’ 49вҶ’## 2. System Architecture Overview 50вҶ’ 51вҶ’### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram 52вҶ’ 53вҶ’```mermaid 54вҶ’graph TB 55вҶ’ subgraph External["External Actors"] 56вҶ’ Admin["рҹ”‘ Admin User"] 57вҶ’ Guest["рҹ‘Ө Guest User"] 58вҶ’ Bot["рҹӨ– Search Engine Bot"] 59вҶ’ end 60вҶ’ 61вҶ’ subgraph Client["Client Layer вҖ” Next.js"] 62вҶ’ AdminUI["Admin Dashboard
(M-CV1)"] 63вҶ’ PublicUI["Public View SSR
(M-GV1)"] 64вҶ’ ClientInt["Client Interaction
(M-GV2)"] 65вҶ’ end 66вҶ’ 67вҶ’ subgraph Server["Server Layer вҖ” Express + tRPC"] 68вҶ’ APIGateway["API Gateway
(M-B1)"] 69вҶ’ AccessCtrl["Access Control
(M-B2)"] 70вҶ’ VisBiz["Visibility Service
(M-B3)"] 71вҶ’ ContentDel["Content Delivery
(M-B4)"] 72вҶ’ MetaTag["Meta Tag Engine
(M-B5)"] 73вҶ’ SEOIndex["SEO & Indexing

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Channel Visibility Toggle
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Public/Indexable Channel Control
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Community Administrator, I want to toggle specific channels as "Public/Indexable" or "Private," so that I can control which parts of my community are exposed to the open web while keeping sensitive social conversations private.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B3** (Visibility Management), **M-B6** (SEO & Indexing, shared), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Guest Public Channel View
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Anonymous Access to Public Channel Content
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Guest User (Searcher), I want to view the full contents of a public channel via a direct URL without being prompted to log in, so that I can get the answer to my specific question immediately without the friction of creating an account I might only use once.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B2** (Access Control), **M-B4** (Content Delivery), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

1вҶ’# Development Specification: SEO Meta Tag Generation
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Automatic HTML Meta Tag Generation for Public Threads
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Content Creator, I want public threads to automatically generate HTML meta tags (Title, Description) based on the conversation content, so that my community appears with relevant previews in Google search results, driving organic growth.

## Comprehensive Backend Overview: Harmony Repository

### 1. **Backend Code Structure**

**Location:** `/Users/allen/repos/Harmony/harmony-backend/`

**Core Directory Organization:**
- `/src/` - TypeScript source code

  - `/services/` - Business logic services (14 services)
  - `/middleware/` - Express middleware (4 files)
  - `/db/` - Database and Redis connections
  - `/lib/` - Utilities and storage providers
  - `/events/` - Event bus and pub/sub system
  - `/dev/` - Development seed scripts
- `/dist/` - Compiled JavaScript output
- `/tests/` - Test suite (29 test files, 6436 lines total)
- `/prisma/` - Database schema and migrations
- `/node_modules/` - Dependencies

---

### 2. **Documentation & Specifications**

**Location:** `/Users/allen/repos/Harmony/docs/`

**Key Documents:**
- `/docs/unified-backend-architecture.md` - **Authoritative reference** for:
  - Module boundaries (M-B1 through M-B7 backend modules)
  - Class hierarchy and entity relationships
  - Data model and ER diagrams
  - tRPC vs REST API split design
  - Event bus architecture
  - Cache strategy and TTLs

**Feature Dev Specs:**
1. `/docs/dev-spec-channel-visibility-toggle.md` - Channel visibility control feature
   - User story: Community admins toggle channels Public/Private
   - Modules: M-B3 (Visibility Management), M-B6 (SEO & Indexing)
   - Contains: Architecture diagram, class diagrams, API specs

2. `/docs/dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md` - Anonymous public access
   - User story: Guest users view public channels without login
   - Modules: M-B2 (Access Control), M-B4 (Content Delivery)
   - Contains: System diagrams, permission models, cache strategies

3. `/docs/dev-spec-seo-meta-tag-generation.md` - SEO meta tags
   - User story: Auto-generate meta tags for search engine indexing
   - Modules: M-B5 (Meta Tag Engine), M-B6 (SEO & Indexing)
   - Contains: Meta tag generation logic, structured data specs

**Backend README:**
- `/harmony-backend/README.md` - Setup, development, and architecture reference

---

### 3. **Test Files (29 test files)**

**Location:** `/Users/allen/repos/Harmony/harmony-backend/tests/`

**Coverage Areas:**
- **Service Tests:** 
  - `channel.service.test.ts`
  - `message.service.test.ts`
  - `user.service.test.ts`
  - `voice.service.test.ts`
  - `auditLog.service.test.ts`
  - `visibility.service.test.ts`
  - `cache.service.test.ts`
  - `permission.service.test.ts`
  - `indexing.service.test.ts`
  - `attachment.service.test.ts`

- **Event Tests:**
  - `eventBus.test.ts`
  - `channel.service.events.test.ts`
  - `message.service.events.test.ts`

- **Router Tests:**
  - `attachment.router.test.ts`
  - `auth.test.ts`
  - `public.router.test.ts`
  - `events.router.test.ts`
  - `events.router.sse-server-updated.test.ts`
  - `events.router.server-updated.test.ts`

- **Middleware Tests:**
  - `rate-limit.middleware.test.ts`
  - `cache.middleware.test.ts`
  - `trpc.permission.middleware.test.ts`
  - `trpc.error-formatter.test.ts`

- **Data Tests:**

  - `demo-seed.test.ts`

- **Integration Tests:**
  - `app.test.ts`
  - `server.test.ts`
  - `serverMember.test.ts`

**Test Configuration:**
- Jest with ts-jest preset
- Node test environment
- dotenv setup for env vars
- ~6,436 lines of test code

---

### 4. **tRPC Router Architecture**

**Main Router:** `/src/trpc/router.ts`
```typescript
appRouter {
  в”ңв”Җ health: publicProcedure.query()
  в”ңв”Җ channel: channelRouter
  в”ңв”Җ server: serverRouter
  в”ңв”Җ serverMember: serverMemberRouter
  в”ңв”Җ message: messageRouter
  в”ңв”Җ user: userRouter
  в”ңв”Җ attachment: attachmentRouter
  в””в”Җ voice: voiceRouter
}
```

**7 tRPC Routers:**
1. `/src/trpc/routers/channel.router.ts` (3.1K) - Channel operations
2. `/src/trpc/routers/server.router.ts` (2.1K) - Server management
3. `/src/trpc/routers/serverMember.router.ts` (1.9K) - Member operations
4. `/src/trpc/routers/message.router.ts` (3.7K) - Message CRUD + SSE
5. `/src/trpc/routers/user.router.ts` (919B) - User profile
6. `/src/trpc/routers/attachment.router.ts` (947B) - File uploads
7. `/src/trpc/routers/voice.router.ts` (4.8K) - Voice channel handling

**Initialization:** `/src/trpc/init.ts`
- TRPCContext interface with userId, ip, userAgent
- Context factory from Express Request
- Error formatter (hides stack in production)
- **Procedure types:**
  - `publicProcedure` - Unauthenticated
  - `authedProcedure` - Requires authentication
  - `withPermission(action)` - RBAC middleware (requires serverId in input)

---

### 5. **REST Routes (Express)**

**5 REST Routers:** `/src/routes/`
1. `/src/routes/auth.router.ts` (4.5K) - Login, register, refresh, logout
2. `/src/routes/public.router.ts` (9.3K) - Public channel/server access
3. `/src/routes/attachment.router.ts` (4.8K) - File uploads/downloads
4. `/src/routes/events.router.ts` (12.2K) - Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time updates
5. `/src/routes/seo.router.ts` (1.7K) - SEO endpoints (robots.txt, sitemap)

---

### 6. **Business Logic Services (14 services)**

**Location:** `/src/services/`

**Core Services:**
1. **`channel.service.ts`** (5.5K)
   - `getChannels(serverId)`
   - `getChannelBySlug(serverSlug, channelSlug)`
   - `createChannel(input)` - Validates visibility rules
   - Channel queries and updates

2. **`message.service.ts`** (11K)
   - `getMessages(serverId, channelId, cursor, limit)` - Paginated retrieval
   - `sendMessage(input)` - Author anonymity rules for public channels
   - `editMessage(input)`, `deleteMessage(input)`
   - Attachment projection
   - Event emission (MESSAGE_CREATED, MESSAGE_EDITED, MESSAGE_DELETED)

3. **`visibility.service.ts`** (4.5K) - **M-B3 Visibility Management**
   - `getVisibility(channelId, serverId)`
   - `setVisibility(input)` - State machine + audit logging
   - Validates visibility transitions
   - Emits VISIBILITY_CHANGED event
   - **No permission checks** (delegated to router via `withPermission`)

4. **`permission.service.ts`** (4.1K) - **M-B2 Access Control**
   - `requirePermission(userId, serverId, action)`
   - `canManageChannel(userId, channelId, serverId)`
   - RBAC checks with ServerMember role lookup

5. **`auditLog.service.ts`** (3.6K)
   - `logVisibilityChange(input)`
   - `getAuditHistory(channelId)`
   - Stores actor, IP, user agent, action, timestamp

6. **`server.service.ts`** (6.4K)
   - `getServer(id)`, `getServerBySlug(slug)`
   - `createServer(input)`, `updateServer(input)`
   - Member count tracking

7. **`user.service.ts`** (3.2K)
   - `getUser(id)`, `getUserByEmail(email)`
   - `createUser(input)`, `updateUser(input)`
   - Password hashing via bcryptjs

8. **`auth.service.ts`** (8.6K)
   - JWT token generation/verification (access + refresh)
   - `registerUser(email, password, displayName)`
   - `loginUser(email, password)`
   - Token rotation

9. **`cache.service.ts`** (3.2K)
   - Redis-backed caching with TTLs
   - Key patterns: `channel:${id}:visibility`, `server:${id}:info`, `meta:channel:${id}`
   - TTLs: visibility 1h, messages 1min, server info 5min
   - Stale-while-revalidate pattern

10. **`cacheInvalidator.service.ts`** (5.1K)
    - Subscribes to events: VISIBILITY_CHANGED, MESSAGE_CREATED, MESSAGE_DELETED
    - Invalidates affected cache keys
    - Fire-and-forget pattern

11. **`attachment.service.ts`** (3.7K) - File storage
    - Local file provider (configurable)
    - MIME type detection
    - URL generation

12. **`voice.service.ts`** (10.6K)
    - Twilio integration
    - Room creation and token generation
    - Call state management

13. **`serverMember.service.ts`** (7.5K)
    - Member list and roles
    - Server membership queries
    - Role assignment

14. **`indexing.service.ts`** (4.1K) - **M-B6 SEO & Indexing**
    - `updateSitemap()` - Generate sitemap.xml
    - `notifySearchEngines(channelId)` - Ping Google/Bing
    - `generateCanonicalUrl(serverSlug, channelSlug)`
    - `getRobotsDirectives(visibility)`

---

### 7. **Middleware (4 files)**

**Location:** `/src/middleware/`

1. **`auth.middleware.ts`** - JWT token extraction and validation
2. **`cors.ts`** - CORS policy configuration
3. **`cache.middleware.ts`** - Response caching for public routes
4. **`rate-limit.middleware.ts`** - Rate limiting for auth endpoints and public APIs
   - Login: 10 attempts / 15 minutes
   - Register: 5 attempts / 1 hour (1000 in dev)
   - Refresh: 30 attempts / 15 minutes

---

### 8. **Database & Event System**

**Database:** PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Location:** `/src/db/prisma.ts`
- Singleton PrismaClient
- Schema: `/prisma/schema.prisma`
- **8 database migrations** in `/prisma/migrations/`
  - Initial schema setup
  - Auth fields
  - Server owner field
  - RBAC and ServerMembers
  - Message pinning
  - User status tracking
  - Missing indexes

**Redis Setup:** `/src/db/redis.ts`
- Shared ioredis client
- Used for: Cache, Pub/Sub event bus

**Event Bus:** `/src/events/eventBus.ts` - **Redis Pub/Sub**
- Event types: `/src/events/eventTypes.ts`
- Channels:
  - `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` - Channel visibility updates
  - `MESSAGE_CREATED`, `MESSAGE_EDITED`, `MESSAGE_DELETED`
  - `METATAGS_UPDATED`
  - `SERVER_UPDATED`
- Design: Lazy subscriber client + shared publisher

---

### 9. **Entity & Data Model**

**Prisma Models (11 core tables):**
- **User** - Accounts, profile, status
- **RefreshToken** - JWT refresh token tracking
- **Server** - Community/guild setup
- **ServerMember** - User roles within servers (OWNER, ADMIN, MODERATOR, MEMBER, GUEST)
- **Channel** - Text/Voice/Announcement channels
- **Message** - Chat messages with attachments
- **Attachment** - File metadata and storage links
- **VisibilityAuditLog** - Visibility change history
- **MetaTag** - Pre-generated SEO meta tags
- **ChannelVisibility Enum** - PUBLIC_INDEXABLE | PUBLIC_NO_INDEX | PRIVATE
- **RoleType Enum** - 5-level RBAC hierarchy

---

### 10. **Development & Configuration**

**Package.json Scripts:**
```
npm run dev         - tsx watch (hot reload)
npm run build       - tsc to dist/
npm run lint        - eslint src tests
npm test            - jest test suite
npm run db:seed:mock    - Prisma seed
npm run db:seed:demo    - Custom demo seed
```

**Key Dependencies:**
- `@trpc/server` ^11.0.0
- `express` ^4.21.2
- `@prisma/client` ^5.22.0
- `ioredis` ^5.10.0
- `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.3
- `bcryptjs` ^3.0.3
- `zod` ^3.24.2
- `helmet` (security), `cors`, `express-rate-limit`

**Node Version:** >=20

---

### 11. **Architecture Highlights**

**tRPC + REST Split:**
- **tRPC** (authenticated) - Admin operations, visibility toggling, audit logs
- **REST** (public) - SEO crawlers, guest access, social-media unfurlers

**Three Feature Layers:**
1. **Channel Visibility Toggle** (M-B3) - Admins control public/private state
2. **Guest Public Channel View** (M-B4) - Guests read public channels without auth
3. **SEO Meta Tag Generation** (M-B5) - Auto-generate meta tags for search engines

**Event-Driven Updates:**
- Visibility changes вҶ’ Cache invalidation вҶ’ Sitemap regeneration вҶ’ Search engine ping
- Message creates/edits/deletes вҶ’ Event broadcast вҶ’ SSE real-time delivery
- All fire-and-forget (non-blocking)

**Permission Model:**
- RBAC with 5 roles (OWNER > ADMIN > MODERATOR > MEMBER > GUEST)
- `withPermission(action)` middleware validates serverId and role
- Authorization at router layer (not service layer)

---

### 12. **Summary Table**

| Component | Files | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|-------|---------|
| **tRPC Routers** | 7 | ~18.5K | Type-safe authenticated APIs |
| **REST Routes** | 5 | ~32.8K | Public SEO + guest access |
| **Services** | 14 | ~89K | Business logic |
| **Middleware** | 4 | ~10.3K | Auth, CORS, caching, rate limits |
| **Tests** | 29 | ~6.4K | Unit, integration, E2E |
| **Database** | Schema + 8 migrations | ~800 lines | PostgreSQL with Prisma |
| **Events** | Event bus + types | ~150 lines | Redis Pub/Sub |
| **Total Backend** | ~60 files | ~200K+ | Production-ready Node/Express/tRPC server |

All backend code follows the unified architecture spec and implements the three feature specifications with proper separation of concerns, error handling, and extensive test coverage.

  - `visibility.service.test.ts`
  - `cache.service.test.ts`
  - `permission.service.test.ts`
  - `indexing.service.test.ts`
  - `attachment.service.test.ts`

- **Event Tests:**
  - `eventBus.test.ts`
  - `channel.service.events.test.ts`
  - `message.service.events.test.ts`

- **Router Tests:**
  - `attachment.router.test.ts`
  - `auth.test.ts`
  - `public.router.test.ts`
  - `events.router.test.ts`
  - `events.router.sse-server-updated.test.ts`
  - `events.router.server-updated.test.ts`

- **Middleware Tests:**
  - `rate-limit.middleware.test.ts`
  - `cache.middleware.test.ts`
  - `trpc.permission.middleware.test.ts`
  - `trpc.error-formatter.test.ts`

- **Data Tests:**
  - `schema.test.ts`
  - `mock-seed.test.ts`
  - `demo-seed.test.ts`

- **Integration Tests:**
  - `app.test.ts`
  - `server.test.ts`
  - `serverMember.test.ts`

**Test Configuration:**
- Jest with ts-jest preset
- Node test environment
- dotenv setup for env vars
- ~6,436 lines of test code

---

### 4. **tRPC Router Architecture**

**Main Router:** `/src/trpc/router.ts`
```typescript
appRouter {
  в”ңв”Җ health: publicProcedure.query()
  в”ңв”Җ channel: channelRouter
  в”ңв”Җ server: serverRouter
  в”ңв”Җ serverMember: serverMemberRouter
  в”ңв”Җ message: messageRouter
  в”ңв”Җ user: userRouter
  в”ңв”Җ attachment: attachmentRouter
  в””в”Җ voice: voiceRouter
}
```

**7 tRPC Routers:**
1. `/src/trpc/routers/channel.router.ts` (3.1K) - Channel operations
2. `/src/trpc/routers/server.router.ts` (2.1K) - Server management
3. `/src/trpc/routers/serverMember.router.ts` (1.9K) - Member operations
4. `/src/trpc/routers/message.router.ts` (3.7K) - Message CRUD + SSE
5. `/src/trpc/routers/user.router.ts` (919B) - User profile
6. `/src/trpc/routers/attachment.router.ts` (947B) - File uploads
7. `/src/trpc/routers/voice.router.ts` (4.8K) - Voice channel handling

**Initialization:** `/src/trpc/init.ts`
- TRPCContext interface with userId, ip, userAgent
- Context factory from Express Request
- Error formatter (hides stack in production)
- **Procedure types:**
  - `publicProcedure` - Unauthenticated
  - `authedProcedure` - Requires authentication
  - `withPermission(action)` - RBAC middleware (requires serverId in input)

---

### 5. **REST Routes (Express)**

**5 REST Routers:** `/src/routes/`
1. `/src/routes/auth.router.ts` (4.5K) - Login, register, refresh, logout
2. `/src/routes/public.router.ts` (9.3K) - Public channel/server access
3. `/src/routes/attachment.router.ts` (4.8K) - File uploads/downloads
4. `/src/routes/events.router.ts` (12.2K) - Server-Sent Events (SSE) for real-time updates
5. `/src/routes/seo.router.ts` (1.7K) - SEO endpoints (robots.txt, sitemap)

---

### 6. **Business Logic Services (14 services)**

**Location:** `/src/services/`

**Core Services:**
1. **`channel.service.ts`** (5.5K)
   - `getChannels(serverId)`
   - `getChannelBySlug(serverSlug, channelSlug)`
   - `createChannel(input)` - Validates visibility rules
   - Channel queries and updates

2. **`message.service.ts`** (11K)
   - `getMessages(serverId, channelId, cursor, limit)` - Paginated retrieval
   - `sendMessage(input)` - Author anonymity rules for public channels
   - `editMessage(input)`, `deleteMessage(input)`
   - Attachment projection
   - Event emission (MESSAGE_CREATED, MESSAGE_EDITED, MESSAGE_DELETED)

3. **`visibility.service.ts`** (4.5K) - **M-B3 Visibility Management**
   - `getVisibility(channelId, serverId)`
   - `setVisibility(input)` - State machine + audit logging
   - Validates visibility transitions
   - Emits VISIBILITY_CHANGED event
   - **No permission checks** (delegated to router via `withPermission`)

4. **`permission.service.ts`** (4.1K) - **M-B2 Access Control**
   - `requirePermission(userId, serverId, action)`
   - `canManageChannel(userId, channelId, serverId)`
   - RBAC checks with ServerMember role lookup

5. **`auditLog.service.ts`** (3.6K)
   - `logVisibilityChange(input)`
   - `getAuditHistory(channelId)`
   - Stores actor, IP, user agent, action, timestamp

6. **`server.service.ts`** (6.4K)
   - `getServer(id)`, `getServerBySlug(slug)`
   - `createServer(input)`, `updateServer(input)`
   - Member count tracking

7. **`user.service.ts`** (3.2K)
   - `getUser(id)`, `getUserByEmail(email)`
   - `createUser(input)`, `updateUser(input)`
   - Password hashing via bcryptjs

8. **`auth.service.ts`** (8.6K)
   - JWT token generation/verification (access + refresh)
   - `registerUser(email, password, displayName)`
   - `loginUser(email, password)`
   - Token rotation

9. **`cache.service.ts`** (3.2K)
   - Redis-backed caching with TTLs
   - Key patterns: `channel:${id}:visibility`, `server:${id}:info`, `meta:channel:${id}`
   - TTLs: visibility 1h, messages 1min, server info 5min
   - Stale-while-revalidate pattern

10. **`cacheInvalidator.service.ts`** (5.1K)
    - Subscribes to events: VISIBILITY_CHANGED, MESSAGE_CREATED, MESSAGE_DELETED
    - Invalidates affected cache keys
    - Fire-and-forget pattern

11. **`attachment.service.ts`** (3.7K) - File storage
    - Local file provider (configurable)
    - MIME type detection
    - URL generation

12. **`voice.service.ts`** (10.6K)
    - Twilio integration
    - Room creation and token generation
    - Call state management

13. **`serverMember.service.ts`** (7.5K)
    - Member list and roles
    - Server membership queries
    - Role assignment

14. **`indexing.service.ts`** (4.1K) - **M-B6 SEO & Indexing**
    - `updateSitemap()` - Generate sitemap.xml
    - `notifySearchEngines(channelId)` - Ping Google/Bing
    - `generateCanonicalUrl(serverSlug, channelSlug)`
    - `getRobotsDirectives(visibility)`

---

### 7. **Middleware (4 files)**

**Location:** `/src/middleware/`

1. **`auth.middleware.ts`** - JWT token extraction and validation
2. **`cors.ts`** - CORS policy configuration
3. **`cache.middleware.ts`** - Response caching for public routes
4. **`rate-limit.middleware.ts`** - Rate limiting for auth endpoints and public APIs
   - Login: 10 attempts / 15 minutes
   - Register: 5 attempts / 1 hour (1000 in dev)
   - Refresh: 30 attempts / 15 minutes

---

### 8. **Database & Event System**

**Database:** PostgreSQL with Prisma ORM
- **Location:** `/src/db/prisma.ts`
- Singleton PrismaClient
- Schema: `/prisma/schema.prisma`
- **8 database migrations** in `/prisma/migrations/`
  - Initial schema setup
  - Auth fields
  - Server owner field
  - RBAC and ServerMembers
  - Message pinning
  - User status tracking
  - Missing indexes

**Redis Setup:** `/src/db/redis.ts`
- Shared ioredis client
- Used for: Cache, Pub/Sub event bus

**Event Bus:** `/src/events/eventBus.ts` - **Redis Pub/Sub**
- Event types: `/src/events/eventTypes.ts`
- Channels:
  - `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` - Channel visibility updates
  - `MESSAGE_CREATED`, `MESSAGE_EDITED`, `MESSAGE_DELETED`
  - `METATAGS_UPDATED`
  - `SERVER_UPDATED`
- Design: Lazy subscriber client + shared publisher

---

### 9. **Entity & Data Model**

**Prisma Models (11 core tables):**
- **User** - Accounts, profile, status
- **RefreshToken** - JWT refresh token tracking
- **Server** - Community/guild setup
- **ServerMember** - User roles within servers (OWNER, ADMIN, MODERATOR, MEMBER, GUEST)
- **Channel** - Text/Voice/Announcement channels
- **Message** - Chat messages with attachments
- **Attachment** - File metadata and storage links
- **VisibilityAuditLog** - Visibility change history
- **MetaTag** - Pre-generated SEO meta tags
- **ChannelVisibility Enum** - PUBLIC_INDEXABLE | PUBLIC_NO_INDEX | PRIVATE
- **RoleType Enum** - 5-level RBAC hierarchy

---

### 10. **Development & Configuration**

**Package.json Scripts:**
```
npm run dev         - tsx watch (hot reload)
npm run build       - tsc to dist/
npm run lint        - eslint src tests
npm test            - jest test suite
npm run db:seed:mock    - Prisma seed
npm run db:seed:demo    - Custom demo seed
```

**Key Dependencies:**
- `@trpc/server` ^11.0.0
- `express` ^4.21.2
- `@prisma/client` ^5.22.0
- `ioredis` ^5.10.0
- `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.3
- `bcryptjs` ^3.0.3
- `zod` ^3.24.2
- `helmet` (security), `cors`, `express-rate-limit`

**Node Version:** >=20

---

### 11. **Architecture Highlights**

**tRPC + REST Split:**
- **tRPC** (authenticated) - Admin operations, visibility toggling, audit logs
- **REST** (public) - SEO crawlers, guest access, social-media unfurlers

**Three Feature Layers:**
1. **Channel Visibility Toggle** (M-B3) - Admins control public/private state
2. **Guest Public Channel View** (M-B4) - Guests read public channels without auth
3. **SEO Meta Tag Generation** (M-B5) - Auto-generate meta tags for search engines

**Event-Driven Updates:**
- Visibility changes вҶ’ Cache invalidation вҶ’ Sitemap regeneration вҶ’ Search engine ping
- Message creates/edits/deletes вҶ’ Event broadcast вҶ’ SSE real-time delivery
- All fire-and-forget (non-blocking)

**Permission Model:**
- RBAC with 5 roles (OWNER > ADMIN > MODERATOR > MEMBER > GUEST)
- `withPermission(action)` middleware validates serverId and role
- Authorization at router layer (not service layer)

---

### 12. **Summary Table**

| Component | Files | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|-------|---------|
| **tRPC Routers** | 7 | ~18.5K | Type-safe authenticated APIs |
| **REST Routes** | 5 | ~32.8K | Public SEO + guest access |
| **Services** | 14 | ~89K | Business logic |
| **Middleware** | 4 | ~10.3K | Auth, CORS, caching, rate limits |
| **Tests** | 29 | ~6.4K | Unit, integration, E2E |
| **Database** | Schema + 8 migrations | ~800 lines | PostgreSQL with Prisma |
| **Events** | Event bus + types | ~150 lines | Redis Pub/Sub |
| **Total Backend** | ~60 files | ~200K+ | Production-ready Node/Express/tRPC server |

All backend code follows the unified architecture spec and implements the three feature specifications with proper separation of concerns, error handling, and extensive test coverage.
Now let me check the actual content of the docs and README to see what's complete vs missing.

# Harmony Backend

Express + tRPC server for the Harmony chat application.

## Architecture

> **Before making changes to this backend, read the unified backend architecture document:**
> [`docs/unified-backend-architecture.md`](../docs/unified-backend-architecture.md)

The architecture doc covers:
- **Module map** вҖ” what each module (M-B1вҖ“M-B7, M-D1вҖ“M-D3) owns and its boundaries
- **Class diagrams** вҖ” all services, repositories, controllers, entities, and DTOs
- **Data model** вҖ” ER diagram for all shared database tables
- **API surface** вҖ” tRPC procedures and public REST endpoints
- **Event bus** вҖ” Redis Pub/Sub event flow between modules
- **Cache strategy** вҖ” Redis key layout and TTLs
- **Security model** вҖ” rate limiting, visibility guards, content filtering

Use the module map (В§2.2) as your entry point when navigating the codebase.

## Development

```bash
npm install
npm run dev       # tsx watch (hot reload)
npm run build     # tsc вҶ’ dist/
npm run lint      # eslint src tests
npm test          # jest
```

## Setting Up Locally

```bash
# 1. Start all required services (Postgres + Redis)
docker compose up -d

# 2. Copy env (only once) вҖ” then edit JWT secrets before running!
cp .env.example .env
# Open .env and set strong, unique values for:
#   JWT_ACCESS_SECRET=<random string, 32+ chars>
#   JWT_REFRESH_SECRET=<random string, 32+ chars>
# The placeholder values are insecure defaults: the server will start with them,
# but anyone could forge tokens. The process hard-crashes on startup only if
# either JWT_ACCESS_SECRET or JWT_REFRESH_SECRET is missing entirely.

# 3. Apply migrations (once, and again after any schema change)
npx prisma migrate deploy

# 4. Run tests
npm test
```

> **Why both Postgres and Redis?**
> Redis is required for visibility caching and the Pub/Sub event bus (see В§4.4 and В§6 of `docs/unified-backend-architecture.md`). Starting only Postgres will disable caching and event-driven features that depend on Redis.

## Environment

Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in values before running locally.

Key variables: `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `FRONTEND_URL`, `PORT` (default `4000`).

> **Security:** `JWT_ACCESS_SECRET` and `JWT_REFRESH_SECRET` in `.env.example` are insecure placeholder values. The server will start with them, but tokens can be forged. Replace them with strong, unique secrets before running the server.

1вҶ’# Unified Backend Architecture вҖ” Harmony 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’> **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 featuresвҖ”**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**вҖ”in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. 4вҶ’ 5вҶ’--- 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’## 1. Design Rationale 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’### 1.2 Key Design Choices 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’| Decision | Choice | Justification | 16вҶ’|----------|--------|---------------| 17вҶ’| **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | 18вҶ’| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | 19вҶ’| **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | 20вҶ’| **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | 21вҶ’| **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | 22вҶ’| **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional serverвҶ’client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | 23вҶ’| **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | 24вҶ’| **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | 25вҶ’| **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | 26вҶ’| **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | 27вҶ’ 28вҶ’### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split 29вҶ’ 30вҶ’``` 31вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ 32вҶ’в”Ӯ API Surface в”Ӯ 33вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө 34вҶ’в”Ӯ tRPC (Authenticated)в”Ӯ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) в”Ӯ 35вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ 36вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Channel settings в”Ӯ вҖў GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) в”Ӯ 37вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Visibility toggle в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/channels/вҖҰ (messages) в”Ӯ 38вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Audit log queries в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/servers/вҖҰ (server) в”Ӯ 39вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Admin meta-tag в”Ӯ вҖў GET /sitemap/{server}.xml в”Ӯ 40вҶ’в”Ӯ overrides в”Ӯ вҖў GET /robots.txt в”Ӯ 41вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў GET /s/{server} (server landing) в”Ӯ 42вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ 43вҶ’``` 44вҶ’ 45вҶ’**Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. 46вҶ’ 47вҶ’--- 48вҶ’ 49вҶ’## 2. System Architecture Overview 50вҶ’ 51вҶ’### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram 52вҶ’ 53вҶ’```mermaid 54вҶ’graph TB 55вҶ’ subgraph External["External Actors"] 56вҶ’ Admin["рҹ”‘ Admin User"] 57вҶ’ Guest["рҹ‘Ө Guest User"] 58вҶ’ Bot["рҹӨ– Search Engine Bot"] 59вҶ’ end 60вҶ’ 61вҶ’ subgraph Client["Client Layer вҖ” Next.js"] 62вҶ’ AdminUI["Admin Dashboard
(M-CV1)"] 63вҶ’ PublicUI["Public View SSR
(M-GV1)"] 64вҶ’ ClientInt["Client Interaction
(M-GV2)"] 65вҶ’ end 66вҶ’ 67вҶ’ subgraph Server["Server Layer вҖ” Express + tRPC"] 68вҶ’ APIGateway["API Gateway
(M-B1)"] 69вҶ’ AccessCtrl["Access Control
(M-B2)"] 70вҶ’ VisBiz["Visibility Service
(M-B3)"] 71вҶ’ ContentDel["Content Delivery
(M-B4)"] 72вҶ’ MetaTag["Meta Tag Engine
(M-B5)"] 73вҶ’ SEOIndex["SEO & Indexing
(M-B6)"] 74вҶ’ BgProcess["Background Workers
(M-B7)"] 75вҶ’ end 76вҶ’ 77вҶ’ subgraph Data["Data Layer"] 78вҶ’ PG[("PostgreSQL")] 79вҶ’ Redis[("Redis

# Unified Backend Architecture вҖ” Harmony > **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 featuresвҖ”**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**вҖ”in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. --- ## 1. Design Rationale ### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. ### 1.2 Key Design Choices | Decision | Choice | Justification | |----------|--------|---------------| | **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | | **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | | **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | | **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | | **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | | **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional serverвҶ’client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | | **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | | **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | | **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | | **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | ### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split ``` в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ API Surface в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ tRPC (Authenticated)в”Ӯ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Channel settings в”Ӯ вҖў GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Visibility toggle в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/channels/вҖҰ (messages) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Audit log queries в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/servers/вҖҰ (server) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Admin meta-tag в”Ӯ вҖў GET /sitemap/{server}.xml в”Ӯ в”Ӯ overrides в”Ӯ вҖў GET /robots.txt в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў GET /s/{server} (server landing) в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ ``` **Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. --- ## 2. System Architecture Overview ### 2.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram ```mermaid graph TB subgraph External["External Actors"] Admin["рҹ”‘ Admin User"] Guest["рҹ‘Ө Guest User"] Bot["рҹӨ– Search Engine Bot"] end subgraph Client["Client Layer вҖ” Next.js"] AdminUI["Admin Dashboard
(M-CV1)"] PublicUI["Public View SSR
(M-GV1)"] ClientInt["Client Interaction
(M-GV2)"] end subgraph Server["Server Layer вҖ” Express + tRPC"] APIGateway["API Gateway
(M-B1)"] AccessCtrl["Access Control
(M-B2)"] VisBiz["Visibility Service
(M-B3)"] ContentDel["Content Delivery
(M-B4)"] MetaTag["Meta Tag Engine
(M-B5)"] SEOIndex["SEO & Indexing
(M-B6)"] BgProcess["Background Workers
(M-B7)"] end subgraph Data["Data Layer"] PG[("PostgreSQL")] Redis[("Redis
Cache + Pub/Sub")] end subgraph ExtSystems["External Systems"] Google["Google Search Console"] Bing["Bing Webmaster API"] end Admin -->|tRPC| AdminUI Guest -->|HTTPS| PublicUI Bot -->|HTTPS| PublicUI AdminUI -->|tRPC| APIGateway PublicUI -->|Internal| APIGateway ClientInt -->|REST| APIGateway APIGateway --> AccessCtrl APIGateway --> VisBiz APIGateway --> ContentDel APIGateway --> MetaTag AccessCtrl --> VisBiz VisBiz --> SEOIndex ContentDel --> MetaTag SEOIndex --> BgProcess

### 2.2 Module Map

The unified backend organizes into **shared backend modules** (prefixed `M-B`) and **data layer modules** (prefixed `M-D`). Client-layer modules are listed for reference only; they are specified in their respective feature dev specs.

| Module ID | Name | Layer | Feature Owner | Purpose |
|-----------|------|-------|---------------|---------|
| *M-CV1* | *Admin Dashboard* | *Client* | *Channel Visibility Toggle* | *Specified in [channel visibility spec](./dev-spec-channel-visibility-toggle.md)* |
| *M-CV2* | *Public Channel Viewer* | *Client* | *Channel Visibility Toggle* | *Specified in [channel visibility spec](./dev-spec-channel-visibility-toggle.md)* |
| *M-GV1* | *Public View (SSR)* | *Client* | *Guest Public Channel View* | *Specified in [guest public channel spec](./dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md)* |
| *M-GV2* | *Client Interaction* | *Client* | *Guest Public Channel View* | *Specified in [guest public channel spec](./dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md)* |
| M-B1 | API Gateway | Server | Shared | tRPC router (authenticated) + REST controllers (public) |
| M-B2 | Access Control | Server | Shared | Visibility guard, content filter, rate limiter, anonymous sessions |
| M-B3 | Visibility Management | Server | Channel Visibility Toggle | Visibility state machine, permission checks, audit logging |
| M-B4 | Content Delivery | Server | Guest Public Channel View | Message retrieval, author privacy, attachment processing |
| M-B5 | Meta Tag Engine | Server | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Meta tag generation, content analysis, OpenGraph, structured data |
| M-B6 | SEO & Indexing | Server | Shared | Sitemap generation, search engine notifications, canonical URLs, robots directives |
| M-B7 | Background Workers | Server | Shared | Async workers for meta-tag regeneration, sitemap rebuilds, search engine pings (Redis Pub/Sub driven) |
| M-D1 | Data Access | Data | Shared | Repositories (Channel, Message, Server, User, Attachment, AuditLog, MetaTag) |
| M-D2 | Persistence | Data | Shared | PostgreSQL schemas (all tables) |
| M-D3 | Cache | Data | Shared | Redis cache schemas and Pub/Sub event channels |

---

## 3. Unified Class Hierarchy

### 3.1 Core Entities

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Server {

        +id: UUID
        +name: string
        +slug: string
        +description: string?
        +iconUrl: string?

    }

    class Channel {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +serverId: UUID
        +name: string
        +slug: string
        +type: ChannelType
        +visibility: ChannelVisibility
        +topic: string?
        +position: number
        +indexedAt: DateTime?
        +createdAt: DateTime
        +updatedAt: DateTime
    }

    class Message {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +authorId: UUID
        +content: string
        +createdAt: DateTime
        +editedAt: DateTime?
        +isDeleted: boolean
    }

    class User {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +username: string
        +displayName: string
        +avatarUrl: string?
        +publicProfile: boolean
        +status: UserStatus
        +createdAt: DateTime
    }

    class Attachment {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +messageId: UUID
        +filename: string
        +url: string
        +contentType: string
        +sizeBytes: number
    }

    class AuditLogEntry {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +actorId: UUID
        +action: string
        +oldValue: JSON
        +newValue: JSON
        +timestamp: DateTime
        +ipAddress: string
        +userAgent: string
    }

    class ServerMember {
        <<entity>>
        +userId: UUID
        +serverId: UUID
        +role: RoleType
        +joinedAt: DateTime
    }

    class GeneratedMetaTags {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +title: string
        +description: string
        +ogTitle: string
        +ogDescription: string
        +ogImage: string?
        +twitterCard: string
        +keywords: string[]
        +structuredData: JSON
        %% keywords is stored as JSON text in PostgreSQL
        +contentHash: string

        +generatedAt: DateTime
        +schemaVersion: number
    }

    Server "1" --> "*" Channel
    Channel "1" --> "*" Message
    Message "*" --> "1" User
    Message "1" --> "*" Attachment

    Channel "1" --> "0..1" GeneratedMetaTags

```

> **Entity methods note:** `isPublic()` and `isIndexable()` are logical helpers shown in older diagrams. Because Prisma returns plain data objects, these **must not** be implemented as class methods on the entity. Implement them as utility functions in the service layer (e.g., `isPublicChannel(channel: Channel): boolean` in `visibility.service.ts`).

### 3.2 Interfaces, Enums & Events

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class IVisibilityToggle {
        <<interface>>
        +setVisibility(channelId, visibility, actorId, ip) VisibilityChangeResult
        +getVisibility(channelId) ChannelVisibility
        +canChangeVisibility(channelId, actorId) boolean
    }

    class IMetaTagGenerator {
        <<interface>>
        +generate(channelId) MetaTagSet
        +validate(tags) ValidationResult
    }

    class ChannelType {
        <<enumeration>>
        TEXT
        VOICE
        ANNOUNCEMENT
    }

    class ChannelVisibility {
        <<enumeration>>
        PUBLIC_INDEXABLE
        PUBLIC_NO_INDEX
        PRIVATE
    }

    class RoleType {
        <<enumeration>>
        OWNER
        ADMIN
        MODERATOR
        MEMBER
        GUEST
    }

    class UserStatus {
        <<enumeration>>
        ONLINE
        IDLE
        DND
        OFFLINE
    }

file.content:
# Development Specification: Channel Visibility Toggle

## Feature: Public/Indexable Channel Control

**User Story:** As a Community Administrator, I want to toggle specific channels as "Public/Indexable" or "Private," so that I can control which parts of my community are exposed to the open web while keeping sensitive social conversations private.

> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B3** (Visibility Management), **M-B6** (SEO & Indexing, shared), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

---

## 1. Header

### 1.1 Version and Date

| Version | Date       | Description                              |
|---------|------------|------------------------------------------|
| 1.0     | 2026-02-12 | Initial development specification        |
| 2.0     | 2026-02-12 | Cross-spec consolidation and fixes       |

### 1.2 Author and Role

| Author        | Role                    | Version |
|---------------|-------------------------|---------|
| Claude (AI)   | Specification Author    | 2.0     |
| dblanc        | Project Lead            | 1.0     |
| AvanishKulkarni | Project Lead | 2.0 |

### 1.3 Rationale
Header with versioning and authors.

content.text:
Based on my review, here's what's missing from the P4 requirements:

---

## Missing / Incomplete Items

### Completely Missing

1. **Reflection essay (500 words)** вҖ” No document exists covering:
   - LLM effectiveness for backend code generation
   - What was wrong with LLM output (easy vs. hard to fix)
   - How you verified the implementation was complete

2. **LLM interaction logs** вҖ” No `logs/` directory or log files anywhere. The assignment requires copy-pasted logs from all LLM interactions with name and version of the LLM.

3. **Single turn-in document** вҖ” The assignment says to submit one document containing all parts. Currently the specs are scattered across separate files.

---

### Backend README Gaps (`harmony-backend/README.md`)

The current README has install/start/test commands but is missing:

- **Stop instructions** вҖ” e.g., `docker compose down`
- **Reset instructions** вҖ” how to wipe the database and flush Redis (required for site reliability engineers)
- **Per-module database breakdown** вҖ” what databases/tables each module (M-B1 through M-B7) creates, reads from, and writes to
- **Full external dependency list** вҖ” twilio, sanitize-html, helmet, cors, express-rate-limit, ioredis, etc. with their purpose

---

### Dev Spec Diagram Format

The dev specs (`dev-spec-channel-visibility-toggle.md`, `dev-spec-guest-public-channel-view.md`) use **ASCII art** diagrams. The requirement says:

> "Have the LLM generate **mermaid diagrams** for any required diagram and add **photos** of those diagrams to your submission."

The unified architecture doc has Mermaid diagrams, but the individual dev specs need them too.

---

### Clarity on Which 2 User Stories

There are **3 dev specs** but P4 requires exactly **2 user stories** (one independent, one dependent on the first). The submission needs to explicitly state which two were chosen for P3/P4.

---

### Combined Startup Commands

The assignment requires: *"Provide the necessary commands to get the frontend & backend servers up & running."* The README only has backend commands вҖ” no frontend startup commands are documented.

---

### Summary

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| Backend code | вң… |
| Tests checked in | вң… |
| Unified architecture doc | вң… |
| Dev specs (harmonized) | вң… (but ASCII diagrams) |
| Module specs (8 points each) | вң… (via unified-backend-architecture.md) |
| Backend README | вҡ пёҸ Incomplete |
| Dev spec Mermaid diagrams | вқҢ Missing |
| Both user stories identified | вқҢ Ambiguous (3 specs exist) |
| Frontend + backend startup commands | вқҢ Incomplete |
| Reflection essay | вқҢ Missing |
| LLM interaction logs | вқҢ Missing |
| Single turn-in document | вқҢ Missing |

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Channel Visibility Toggle
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Public/Indexable Channel Control
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Community Administrator, I want to toggle specific channels as "Public/Indexable" or "Private," so that I can control which parts of my community are exposed to the open web while keeping sensitive social conversations private.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B3** (Visibility Management), **M-B6** (SEO & Indexing, shared), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

# Development Specification: Channel Visibility Toggle

## Feature: Public/Indexable Channel Control

**User Story:** As a Community Administrator, I want to toggle specific channels as "Public/Indexable" or "Private," so that I can control which parts of my community are exposed to the open web while keeping sensitive social conversations private.

> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B3** (Visibility Management), **M-B6** (SEO & Indexing, shared), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

---

## 1. Header

### 1.1 Version and Date

| Version | Date       | Description                              |
|---------|------------|------------------------------------------|
| 1.0     | 2026-02-12 | Initial development specification        |
| 2.0     | 2026-02-12 | Cross-spec consolidation and fixes       |

### 1.2 Author and Role

| Author        | Role                    | Version |
|---------------|-------------------------|---------|
| Claude (AI)   | Specification Author    | 2.0     |
| dblanc        | Project Lead            | 1.0     |
| AvanishKulkarni | Project Lead | 2.0 |

### 1.3 Rationale
Header with versioning and authors.

1вҶ’# Unified Backend Architecture вҖ” Harmony 2вҶ’ 3вҶ’> **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 featuresвҖ”**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**вҖ”in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. 4вҶ’ 5вҶ’--- 6вҶ’ 7вҶ’## 1. Design Rationale 8вҶ’ 9вҶ’### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? 10вҶ’ 11вҶ’Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. 12вҶ’ 13вҶ’### 1.2 Key Design Choices 14вҶ’ 15вҶ’| Decision | Choice | Justification | 16вҶ’|----------|--------|---------------| 17вҶ’| **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | 18вҶ’| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | 19вҶ’| **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | 20вҶ’| **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | 21вҶ’| **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | 22вҶ’| **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional serverвҶ’client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | 23вҶ’| **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | 24вҶ’| **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | 25вҶ’| **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | 26вҶ’| **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | 27вҶ’ 28вҶ’### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split 29вҶ’ 30вҶ’``` 31вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ 32вҶ’в”Ӯ API Surface в”Ӯ 33вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө 34вҶ’в”Ӯ tRPC (Authenticated)в”Ӯ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) в”Ӯ 35вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ 36вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Channel settings в”Ӯ вҖў GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) в”Ӯ 37вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Visibility toggle в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/channels/вҖҰ (messages) в”Ӯ 38вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Audit log queries в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/servers/вҖҰ (server) в”Ӯ 39вҶ’в”Ӯ вҖў Admin meta-tag в”Ӯ вҖў GET /sitemap/{server}.xml в”Ӯ 40вҶ’в”Ӯ overrides в”Ӯ вҖў GET /robots.txt в”Ӯ 41вҶ’в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў GET /s/{server} (server landing) в”Ӯ 42вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ 43вҶ’``` 44вҶ’ 45вҶ’**Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. 46вҶ’ 47вҶ’--- 48вҶ’ 49вҶ’## 2. # Unified Backend Architecture вҖ” Harmony > **Scope:** This document specifies the shared backend that powers all three P3 featuresвҖ”**Channel Visibility Toggle**, **Guest Public Channel View**, and **SEO Meta Tag Generation**вҖ”in a single, cohesive service layer. It is the authoritative reference for module boundaries, data models, APIs, and class ownership. --- ## 1. Design Rationale ### 1.1 Why a Unified Backend? Each feature spec was authored independently and defines its own modules, classes, and schemas. Left unmerged, the codebase would contain three competing `ChannelRepository` classes, duplicate cache logic, and inconsistent database schemas. A unified backend eliminates this redundancy while preserving each feature's domain-specific logic. ### 1.2 Key Design Choices | Decision | Choice | Justification | |----------|--------|---------------| | **Primary Language** | TypeScript 5.3+ | End-to-end type safety (client + server); single language reduces context-switching. | | **Database** | PostgreSQL 16+ | ACID guarantees for visibility state transitions; native `ENUM` types for visibility; `JSONB` for flexible audit payloads; partial indexes for efficient public-channel queries. | | **Cache / EventBus** | Redis 7.2+ | Sub-millisecond reads for visibility checks on every public page load; Pub/Sub for cross-module event propagation (`VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_CREATED`, etc.) without tight coupling. | | **Authenticated APIs** | tRPC 11 | End-to-end type inference between Next.js client and Express server; eliminates hand-written API clients for admin operations. | | **Public APIs** | REST (Express) | Search-engine crawlers, social-media link unfurlers, and external consumers require plain HTTP. tRPC's binary protocol is invisible to these consumers. | | **Real-time Transport** | SSE (Server-Sent Events) | Native browser `EventSource` requires no client library; unidirectional serverвҶ’client push fits message delivery perfectly; works over existing HTTP infrastructure with no WebSocket upgrade negotiation. | | **ORM** | Prisma 5.8+ | Type-safe schema definitions; auto-generated migrations; integrates with PostgreSQL enums. | | **Runtime Validation** | Zod 3.22+ | Composes with tRPC for automatic request/response validation; shared between client and server. | | **SSR Framework** | Next.js 14+ | Server-side rendering is critical for SEO; server components reduce client bundle for public pages. | | **HTML Sanitization** | sanitize-html 2.12+ | XSS prevention for user-generated content rendered on public pages. Node.js-native (no DOM dependency). | ### 1.3 tRPC + REST Split ``` в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ API Surface в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө в”Ӯ tRPC (Authenticated)в”Ӯ REST (Public/Unauthenticated) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Channel settings в”Ӯ вҖў GET /c/{server}/{channel} (SSR page) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Visibility toggle в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/channels/вҖҰ (messages) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Audit log queries в”Ӯ вҖў GET /api/public/servers/вҖҰ (server) в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў Admin meta-tag в”Ӯ вҖў GET /sitemap/{server}.xml в”Ӯ в”Ӯ overrides в”Ӯ вҖў GET /robots.txt в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ вҖў GET /s/{server} (server landing) в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ ``` **Why the split?** Crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) and social-media unfurlers (Facebook, Twitter/X, Slack) make standard HTTP requests. They cannot consume tRPC. Admin operations (visibility toggling, meta-tag overrides) benefit from tRPC's type inference and are only used by authenticated Harmony clients. --- ## 2.    162вҶ’This follows a clear model-view-controller architecture, where the client can view channels and control their visibility state with the M1 Admin Dashboard controller. The underlying model is represented by the server layer, which handles get/set operations and any necessary side-effects for search engine indexing.

   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’> **Sitemap Ownership:** `IndexingService` (CL6.1 / C5.2) is the canonical owner of sitemap generation and search engine notification across all Harmony specs. Other features (e.g., SEO Meta Tag Generation) emit events that this service consumes to trigger sitemap updates.
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’### 3.1 Rationale
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’After having an LLM review this spec, the canonical owner of the sitemap generation should be the IndexingService. There was a discrepancy between this spec and the seo-meta-tag-generation spec on what would consume server updates and generate new sitemaps for external services. 
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’Significant inconsistencies existed between section 2, 3, 9, and 10, so a verification pass was necessary after the document was generated to fix them.

   342вҶ’
   343вҶ’### 4.7 Rationale
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’Like the previous section, I had to reprompt to fix inconsistencies. The LLM also noticed that the ChannelRepository interface/class has discrepancies across each spec, so it consolidated each of them together. The class diagrams correctly display the interactions between each class, so no update was needed there.

file.content:
| Flow ID | Source | Destination | Data | Protocol |
|---------|--------|-------------|------|----------|
| F1 | C1.2 VisibilityToggle | C4.1 ChannelController | VisibilityUpdateRequest | HTTPS (tRPC) |
| F2 | C4.1 ChannelController | C5.1 VisibilityService | VisibilityChangeCommand | Internal Call |
| F3 | C5.1 VisibilityService | C6.1 ChannelRepository | Channel Entity | Database Protocol |
| F4 | C5.1 VisibilityService | C5.2 IndexingService | IndexingEvent | EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub) |
| F5 | C5.2 IndexingService | E1 Search Engines | Sitemap XML | HTTPS |
| F6 | C4.2 PublicAccessCtrl | E3 CDN | Cached Public Content | HTTPS |
| F7 | C5.1 VisibilityService | C5.4 AuditLogService | AuditEntry | Internal Call |

### 2.3 Rationale

This follows a clear model-view-controller architecture, where the client can view channels and control their visibility state with the M1 Admin Dashboard controller. The underlying model is represented by the server layer, which handles get/set operations and any necessary side-effects for search engine indexing. 

The underlying data layer uses a short-term caching layer to reduce database accesses and syncs with external systems. 

We had to prompt edits to this to ensure the database columns were not mismatched across each architecture diagram. The Redis cache key pattern was also inconsistent across specs, so we had to prompt fixes for that as well.

---

```

> **Sitemap Ownership:** `IndexingService` (CL6.1 / C5.2) is the canonical owner of sitemap generation and search engine notification across all Harmony specs. Other features (e.g., SEO Meta Tag Generation) emit events that this service consumes to trigger sitemap updates.

### 3.1 Rationale

After having an LLM review this spec, the canonical owner of the sitemap generation should be the IndexingService. There was a discrepancy between this spec and the seo-meta-tag-generation spec on what would consume server updates and generate new sitemaps for external services. 

Significant inconsistencies existed between section 2, 3, 9, and 10, so a verification pass was necessary after the document was generated to fix them. 

---

## 4. List of Classes

### 4.1 Client Module (M1, M2, M3)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-C1.1 | ChannelSettingsView | View Component | Channel settings page with visibility management |
| CL-C1.2 | VisibilityToggleComponent | UI Component | Toggle control for Public/Indexable вҶ” Private with confirmation |
| CL-C2.1 | PublicChannelView | View Component | Public channel content for anonymous users and crawlers |
| CL-C2.2 | MessageListComponent | UI Component | Paginated message list with SEO-optimized markup |
| CL-C3.1 | ChannelService | Service | Client-side channel API calls including visibility updates |
| CL-C3.2 | AuthService | Service | Authentication state and permission checking |

### 4.2 API Gateway Module (M4)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-C4.1 | ChannelController | Controller | Authenticated channel management API (tRPC) |
| CL-C4.2 | PublicAccessController | Controller | Unauthenticated public content and sitemaps (REST) |

### 4.3 Business Logic Module (M5)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-C5.1 | ChannelVisibilityService | Service | Visibility state changes, validation, and event emission |
| CL-C5.2 | IndexingService | Service | Sitemap generation, crawler notifications (canonical owner) |
| CL-C5.3 | PermissionService | Service | User permission validation for channel management |
| CL-C5.4 | AuditLogService | Service | Audit trail for visibility changes |

### 4.4 Data Access Module (M6)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-C6.1 | ChannelRepository | Repository | Channel data access with caching |
| CL-C6.2 | AuditLogRepository | Repository | Audit log data access |

### 4.5 Data Structures (Entities/DTOs)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-D1 | Channel | Entity | Domain entity representing a channel with visibility state |
| CL-D2 | AuditLogEntry | Entity | Single audit log record |
| CL-D3 | VisibilityChangeEvent | Event | Event emitted on visibility changes |
| CL-D4 | ChannelVisibility | Enumeration | Possible visibility states |
| CL-D5 | VisibilityUpdateRequest | DTO | Request payload for visibility update |
| CL-D6 | VisibilityUpdateResponse | DTO | Response payload for visibility update |
| CL-D7 | PublicChannelDTO | DTO | Public-facing channel data (see В§4.6) |

### 4.6 PublicChannelDTO Fields

```typescript
interface PublicChannelDTO {
  id: string;           // Channel UUID
  name: string;         // Display name
  slug: string;         // URL-safe identifier
  topic: string;        // Channel topic/description
  messageCount: number; // Total messages in channel
  serverSlug: string;   // Parent server's slug
}
```

### 4.7 Rationale

Like the previous section, I had to reprompt to fix inconsistencies. The LLM also noticed that the ChannelRepository interface/class has discrepancies across each spec, so it consolidated each of them together. The class diagrams correctly display the interactions between each class, so no update was needed there. 

---

## 5. State Diagrams

        +createdAt: DateTime
        +updatedAt: DateTime
    }

    class Message {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +authorId: UUID
        +content: string
        +createdAt: DateTime
        +editedAt: DateTime?
        +isDeleted: boolean
    }

    class User {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +username: string
        +displayName: string
        +avatarUrl: string?
        +publicProfile: boolean
        +status: UserStatus
        +createdAt: DateTime
    }

    class Attachment {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +messageId: UUID
        +filename: string
        +url: string
        +contentType: string
        +sizeBytes: number
    }

    class AuditLogEntry {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +actorId: UUID
        +action: string
        +oldValue: JSON
        +newValue: JSON
        +timestamp: DateTime
        +ipAddress: string
        +userAgent: string
    }

    class ServerMember {
        <<entity>>
        +userId: UUID
        +serverId: UUID
        +role: RoleType
        +joinedAt: DateTime
    }

    class GeneratedMetaTags {
        <<entity>>
        +id: UUID
        +channelId: UUID
        +title: string
        +description: string
        +ogTitle: string
        +ogDescription: string
        +ogImage: string?
        +twitterCard: string
        +keywords: string[]
        +structuredData: JSON
        %% keywords is stored as JSON text in PostgreSQL
        +contentHash: string
        +needsRegeneration: boolean
        +generatedAt: DateTime
        +schemaVersion: number
    }

    Server "1" --> "*" Channel
    Channel "1" --> "*" Message
    Message "*" --> "1" User
    Message "1" --> "*" Attachment
    Channel "1" --> "*" AuditLogEntry
    Channel "1" --> "0..1" GeneratedMetaTags
    Server "1" --> "*" ServerMember
    User "1" --> "*" ServerMember
```

> **Entity methods note:** `isPublic()` and `isIndexable()` are logical helpers shown in older diagrams. Because Prisma returns plain data objects, these **must not** be implemented as class methods on the entity. Implement them as utility functions in the service layer (e.g., `isPublicChannel(channel: Channel): boolean` in `visibility.service.ts`).

### 3.2 Interfaces, Enums & Events

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class IVisibilityToggle {
        <<interface>>
        +setVisibility(channelId, visibility, actorId, ip) VisibilityChangeResult
        +getVisibility(channelId) ChannelVisibility
        +canChangeVisibility(channelId, actorId) boolean
    }

    class IMetaTagGenerator {
        <<interface>>
        +generate(channelId) MetaTagSet
        +validate(tags) ValidationResult
    }

    class ChannelType {
        <<enumeration>>
        TEXT
        VOICE
        ANNOUNCEMENT
    }

    class ChannelVisibility {
        <<enumeration>>
        PUBLIC_INDEXABLE
        PUBLIC_NO_INDEX
        PRIVATE
    }

    class RoleType {
        <<enumeration>>
        OWNER
        ADMIN
        MODERATOR
        MEMBER
        GUEST
    }

    class UserStatus {
        <<enumeration>>
        ONLINE
        IDLE
        DND
        OFFLINE
    }

    class VisibilityChangeEvent {
        <<event>>
        +channelId: UUID
        +oldVisibility: ChannelVisibility
        +newVisibility: ChannelVisibility
        +actorId: UUID
        +timestamp: DateTime
    }

    VisibilityChangeEvent --> ChannelVisibility
```

   439вҶ’                                       в”Ӯ After 3s / dismiss
   440вҶ’                                       в–ј
   441вҶ’                      в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   442вҶ’                      в”Ӯ A1: Settings Page (clean)     в”Ӯ
   443вҶ’                      в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   444вҶ’```
   445вҶ’
   446вҶ’### 5.4 Rationale
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’The first diagram correctly tracks the state changes for all possible channel states. No changes or reprompting the LLM was necessary for this section. The channel will be public, public & indexable, or private. A simplification of the roles-based access control in Discord, but covers the general idea. 
   449вҶ’
   450вҶ’The second diagram correctly tracks the state transitions for the channel visibility permission. It is quite simple so the model did not need to the reprompted for any changes.
   451вҶ’
   452вҶ’---
   453вҶ’
   454вҶ’## 6. Flow Charts
   455вҶ’
   456вҶ’### 6.1 Scenario: Admin Sets Channel to Public/Indexable
   457вҶ’
   458вҶ’Admin navigates to channel settings and toggles a private channel to publicly indexable. System validates permissions, updates DB, regenerates sitemap, and notifies search engines.
   459вҶ’
   460вҶ’```
   461вҶ’    (( START: Admin opens channel settings ))
   462вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   463вҶ’                        в–ј
   464вҶ’        [F1.1] Load channel data вҶ’ Client.ChannelService.getChannel(channelId)
   465вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   466вҶ’                        в–ј

   469вҶ’                        в–ј

   471вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   472вҶ’                        в–ј
   473вҶ’        [F1.3] Show confirmation dialog
   474вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   475вҶ’                        в–ј

   477вҶ’               / No            Yes \
   478вҶ’              в–ј                     в–ј
   479вҶ’    [F1.5] Cancel        [F1.6] Send API request:
   480вҶ’    Return to settings     updateVisibility(channelId, PUBLIC_INDEXABLE)
   481вҶ’                                    в”Ӯ
   482вҶ’                                    в–ј

   484вҶ’                                    в”Ӯ
   485вҶ’                                    в–ј
   486вҶ’                            < Token valid? >
   487вҶ’                           / No         Yes \
   488вҶ’                          в–ј                  в–ј
   489вҶ’                    Return 401      [F1.10] Check admin permission
   490вҶ’                                             в”Ӯ
   491вҶ’                                             в–ј
   492вҶ’                                    < Has permission? >
   493вҶ’                                   / No             Yes \
   494вҶ’                                  в–ј                      в–ј
   495вҶ’                            Return 403      [F1.13] Update visibility in DB
   496вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ
   497вҶ’                                                         в–ј
   498вҶ’                                            [F1.14] Create audit log entry
   499вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ
   500вҶ’                                                         в–ј
   501вҶ’                                            [F1.15] Emit VISIBILITY_CHANGED event
   502вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ

   505вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ

   508вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ

   511вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ

   514вҶ’                                                         в”Ӯ
