   517вҶ’                                           / No              Yes \
   518вҶ’                                          в–ј                       в–ј
   519вҶ’                                [F1.22] Show error    [F1.23] Update UI toggle
   520вҶ’                                                               в”Ӯ

   522вҶ’                                            (( END: Channel is now public ))
   523вҶ’```
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’#### 6.1.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PUBLIC_INDEXABLE
   526вҶ’
   527вҶ’When visibility changes to `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`:
   528вҶ’1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
   529вҶ’2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event вҶ’ generates meta tags for the channel
   530вҶ’3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ warms guest view cache
   531вҶ’
   532вҶ’### 6.2 Scenario: Anonymous User Views Public Channel
   533вҶ’
   534вҶ’An anonymous user or crawler requests a public channel page. System verifies visibility and serves content with appropriate SEO headers.
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’```

   538вҶ’                        в”Ӯ

   541вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   542вҶ’                        в–ј
   543вҶ’        [F2.2] Check cache: channel:{id}:visibility
   544вҶ’                        в”Ӯ
   545вҶ’                < Cache hit? >
   546вҶ’               / No         Yes \
   547вҶ’              в–ј                  в–ј
   548вҶ’    [F2.4] Query DB     [F2.5] Use cached value

                            в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

```

### 5.4 Rationale

The first diagram correctly tracks the state changes for all possible channel states. No changes or reprompting the LLM was necessary for this section. The channel will be public, public & indexable, or private. A simplification of the roles-based access control in Discord, but covers the general idea. 

The second diagram correctly tracks the state transitions for the channel visibility permission. It is quite simple so the model did not need to the reprompted for any changes.

---

## 6. Flow Charts

### 6.1 Scenario: Admin Sets Channel to Public/Indexable

Admin navigates to channel settings and toggles a private channel to publicly indexable. System validates permissions, updates DB, regenerates sitemap, and notifies search engines.

```

                        в”Ӯ

                        в–ј
        / Admin clicks "Make Public" toggle /

                        в–ј
        [F1.3] Show confirmation dialog
                        в”Ӯ
                        в–ј
                < Admin confirms? >
               / No            Yes \
              в–ј                     в–ј
    [F1.5] Cancel        [F1.6] Send API request:
    Return to settings     updateVisibility(channelId, PUBLIC_INDEXABLE)

                    Return 401      [F1.10] Check admin permission
                                             в”Ӯ

                                   / No             Yes \
                                  в–ј                      в–ј
                            Return 403      [F1.13] Update visibility in DB
                                                         в”Ӯ
                                                         в–ј

                                                         в”Ӯ

                                                         в”Ӯ

                                                         в”Ӯ

                                                         в”Ӯ
                                                         в–ј
                                            < Success response? >
                                           / No              Yes \
                                          в–ј                       в–ј
                                [F1.22] Show error    [F1.23] Update UI toggle
                                                               в”Ӯ

```

#### 6.1.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PUBLIC_INDEXABLE

When visibility changes to `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`:
1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event вҶ’ generates meta tags for the channel
3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ warms guest view cache

### 6.2 Scenario: Anonymous User Views Public Channel

An anonymous user or crawler requests a public channel page. System verifies visibility and serves content with appropriate SEO headers.

   615вҶ’#### 6.3.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PRIVATE
   616вҶ’
   617вҶ’When visibility changes to `PRIVATE`:
   618вҶ’1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
   619вҶ’2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event вҶ’ deletes meta tags for the channel
   620вҶ’3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ invalidates guest view cache
   621вҶ’
   622вҶ’---
   623вҶ’
   624вҶ’### 6.4 Rationale
   625вҶ’
   626вҶ’The LLM had to be reprompted here to clarify which protocols (RPC vs REST) would be used for communications. This was an issue across the this whole dev spec. It was determined REST protocols would be used for public APIs and RPC for internal communications. 
   627вҶ’
   628вҶ’The LLM also had to be reprompted to finalize what the event bus would be. It chose Redis Pub/Sub to allow for visibility change updates to propagate. The cache keying also needed to be updated to match earlier updates. 
   629вҶ’
   630вҶ’## 7. Development Risks and Failures
   631вҶ’
   632вҶ’### 7.1 Runtime Failures
   633вҶ’
   634вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   635вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   636вҶ’| RF-1 | API Server crash | Toggle action fails | Auto-restart; client retries | Medium | Medium |
   637вҶ’| RF-2 | Lost runtime state | Stale visibility displayed | Cache invalidation on recovery | Low | Low |
   638вҶ’| RF-3 | Database corruption | Incorrect visibility; privacy breach | Restore from backup; audit reconciliation | Very Low | Critical |
   639вҶ’| RF-4 | Invalid state transition | Channel may become public unintentionally | Server-side transition validation | Low | High |
   640вҶ’| RF-5 | RPC failure | "Network error" shown | Retry with exponential backoff; circuit breaker | Medium | Medium |
   641вҶ’| RF-6 | Server overload | Slow response or timeout | Rate limiting; horizontal scaling | Medium | Medium |
   642вҶ’| RF-7 | Out of RAM | Server unresponsive | Memory limits; vertical scaling | Low | High |
   643вҶ’| RF-8 | Database out of space | Write operations fail | Storage alerts; archive old audit logs | Low | High |
   644вҶ’
   645вҶ’### 7.2 Connectivity Failures
   646вҶ’
   647вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   648вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   649вҶ’| CF-1 | Lost network | "Connection lost" banner | Auto-reconnect with backoff | Medium | Medium |
   650вҶ’| CF-2 | Lost DB connection | API returns 503 | Connection pool health checks; failover | Low | High |
   651вҶ’| CF-3 | Traffic spike | Increased latency | CDN caching; auto-scaling | Medium | Medium |
   652вҶ’| CF-4 | Search engine API down | Indexing updates delayed | Queue failed notifications; retry | Medium | Low |
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’### 7.3 Hardware Failures
   655вҶ’
   656вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   657вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   658вҶ’| HF-1 | App server down | Service unavailable | Multi-AZ deployment; LB health checks | Low | High |
   659вҶ’| HF-2 | Bad config loaded | Unpredictable behavior | Config validation on startup; rollback | Low | High |
   660вҶ’| HF-3 | System relocation | Temporary outage | Blue-green deployment; DNS TTL management | Very Low | Medium |
   661вҶ’
   662вҶ’### 7.4 Security Failures
   663вҶ’
   664вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   665вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   666вҶ’| IF-1 | DDoS attack | Service degradation | CloudFlare DDoS protection; rate limiting | Medium | High |
   667вҶ’| IF-2 | OS compromise | Full system breach | Incident response; rebuild from clean images | Very Low | Critical |
   668вҶ’| IF-3 | Code tampering | Malicious behavior | Code signing; integrity monitoring | Very Low | Critical |
   669вҶ’| IF-4 | Database theft | Privacy breach | Encryption at rest; access logging | Low | Critical |
   670вҶ’| IF-5 | Bot spam | Public channels flooded | CAPTCHA; rate limiting; content moderation | Medium | Medium |
   671вҶ’| IF-6 | Session hijacking | Unauthorized changes | Secure cookies; session binding; anomaly detection | Low | High |
   672вҶ’
   673вҶ’### 7.5 Failure Priority Matrix
   674вҶ’
   675вҶ’```
   676вҶ’                Impact:  Low       Medium      High        Critical
   677вҶ’           в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   678вҶ’  High     в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ CF-1      в”Ӯ IF-1      в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   679вҶ’  Medium   в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ RF-1,RF-5 в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   680вҶ’           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ RF-6,CF-3 в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   681вҶ’  Low      в”Ӯ RF-2      в”Ӯ HF-3      в”Ӯ RF-7,RF-8 в”Ӯ RF-3,IF-4      в”Ӯ
   682вҶ’           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ CF-2,HF-1 в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   683вҶ’           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ HF-2,IF-6 в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ
   684вҶ’  Very Low в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ IF-2,IF-3      в”Ӯ
   685вҶ’           в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   686вҶ’```
   687вҶ’
   688вҶ’### 7.6 Rationale
   689вҶ’
   690вҶ’Minor reprompting was needed to standardize the rate-limiting policy. It did not affect this section, but other specs mentioned rate-limiting so it had to be added to this one as a failure. Otherwise the failure modes and resolutions make sense, and there are no obvious gaps in errors.
   691вҶ’
   692вҶ’---
   693вҶ’
   694вҶ’## 8. Technology Stack
   695вҶ’
   696вҶ’| Label | Technology | Version | Purpose | Source |
   697вҶ’|-------|------------|---------|---------|-------|
   698вҶ’| T1 | TypeScript | 5.3+ | Primary language (client + server) | https://www.typescriptlang.org/ |
   699вҶ’| T2 | React | 18.2+ | Frontend UI framework | https://react.dev/ |
   700вҶ’| T3 | Next.js | 14.0+ | SSR/SSG framework (SEO-critical for public pages) | https://nextjs.org/ |
   701вҶ’| T4 | Node.js | 20 LTS | Server runtime | https://nodejs.org/ |
   702вҶ’| T5 | PostgreSQL | 16+ | Primary database (ACID, JSONB, enums) | https://www.postgresql.org/ |
   703вҶ’| T6 | Redis | 7.2+ | Caching, session storage, EventBus (Pub/Sub) | https://redis.io/ |
   704вҶ’| T7 | Prisma | 5.8+ | Type-safe ORM with migrations | https://www.prisma.io/ |
   705вҶ’| T8 | tRPC | 10.45+ | End-to-end typesafe APIs (authenticated internal) | https://trpc.io/ |
   706вҶ’| T9 | Zod | 3.22+ | Runtime schema validation (integrates with tRPC) | https://zod.dev/ |
   707вҶ’| T10 | TailwindCSS | 3.4+ | Utility-first CSS framework | https://tailwindcss.com/ |
   708вҶ’| T11 | CloudFlare | N/A | CDN and DDoS protection | https://www.cloudflare.com/ |
   709вҶ’| T12 | Docker | 24+ | Containerization | https://www.docker.com/ |
   710вҶ’| T13 | Google Search Console API | v1 | Programmatic indexing/de-indexing | https://developers.google.com/webmaster-tools |
   711вҶ’| T14 | Bing Webmaster API | v1 | Microsoft search engine integration | https://www.bing.com/webmasters |
   712вҶ’| T15 | Jest | 29+ | Unit/integration testing | https://jestjs.io/ |
   713вҶ’| T16 | Playwright | 1.40+ | Cross-browser E2E testing | https://playwright.dev/ |
   714вҶ’| T17 | sanitize-html | 2.12+ | XSS prevention / HTML sanitization for public-facing content (Node.js-native) | https://github.com/apostrophecms/sanitize-html |
   715вҶ’
   716вҶ’> **Convention:** tRPC is used for authenticated internal APIs between client and server. Public-facing endpoints (sitemaps, public channel pages, robots.txt) use REST for maximum compatibility with crawlers and third-party consumers.
   717вҶ’
   718вҶ’### 8.1 EventBus
   719вҶ’
   720вҶ’**Technology:** Redis Pub/Sub (T6)
   721вҶ’
   722вҶ’Event types consumed/produced across specs:
   723вҶ’
   724вҶ’| Event | Source Spec | Description |
   725вҶ’|-------|-------------|-------------|
   726вҶ’| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | Channel Visibility Toggle (this spec) | Emitted when channel visibility state changes |
   727вҶ’| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | New message in a public channel |
   728вҶ’| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message edited in a public channel |
   729вҶ’| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message deleted from a public channel |
   730вҶ’| `META_TAGS_UPDATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Meta tags regenerated for a channel |
   731вҶ’
   732вҶ’### 8.2 Rationale
   733вҶ’
   734вҶ’Significant reprompting was necessary here because of conflicting tech stacks across each spec. This spec was missing DOMPurify, which would be needed to sanitize and generate sitemaps with other public content. The communication APIs being a mix of RPC and REST was also detected by the LLM here, requiring prompting to fix it. The LLM then determined to use RPC for authenticated internal APIs, while public endpoints would be REST for compatibility with web indexers.
   735вҶ’
   736вҶ’Finally, the LLM made a shared tech-stack document that would be used across each spec. 
   737вҶ’
   738вҶ’---
   739вҶ’
   740вҶ’## 9. APIs
   741вҶ’
   742вҶ’### 9.1 Module M4: API Gateway
   743вҶ’
   744вҶ’#### 9.1.1 CL-C4.1 ChannelController
   745вҶ’
   746вҶ’**Public Methods (Authenticated, tRPC):**
   747вҶ’
   748вҶ’```typescript
   749вҶ’// Get channel settings including visibility
   750вҶ’getChannelSettings(
   751вҶ’  channelId: string,          // UUID
   752вҶ’  context: AuthenticatedContext
   753вҶ’): Promise<ChannelSettingsResponse>
   754вҶ’
   755вҶ’// Update channel visibility
   756вҶ’updateChannelVisibility(
   757вҶ’  channelId: string,
   758вҶ’  body: VisibilityUpdateRequest,  // { visibility: ChannelVisibility }
   759вҶ’  context: AuthenticatedContext
   760вҶ’): Promise<VisibilityUpdateResponse>
   761вҶ’
   762вҶ’// Get visibility change audit history
   763вҶ’getVisibilityAuditLog(
   764вҶ’  channelId: string,
   765вҶ’  query: AuditLogQuery,       // { limit?, offset?, startDate? }
   766вҶ’  context: AuthenticatedContext
   767вҶ’): Promise<AuditLogResponse>
   768вҶ’```
   769вҶ’
   770вҶ’**Private Methods:**
   771вҶ’
   772вҶ’```typescript
   773вҶ’private validateAdminAccess(userId: string, channelId: string): Promise<boolean>
   774вҶ’private mapToResponse(channel: Channel): ChannelSettingsResponse
   775вҶ’```
   776вҶ’
   777вҶ’#### 9.1.2 CL-C4.2 PublicAccessController
   778вҶ’
   779вҶ’**Public Methods (Unauthenticated, REST):**
   780вҶ’
   781вҶ’```typescript
   782вҶ’// GET /c/{serverSlug}/{channelSlug}
   783вҶ’getPublicChannel(
   784вҶ’  serverSlug: string, channelSlug: string, query: PaginationQuery
   785вҶ’): Promise<PublicChannelPage>
   786вҶ’
   787вҶ’// GET /sitemap/{serverSlug}.xml
   788вҶ’getServerSitemap(serverSlug: string): Promise<SitemapXML>
   789вҶ’
   790вҶ’// GET /robots.txt
   791вҶ’getRobotsTxt(): Promise<RobotsTxt>
   792вҶ’
   793вҶ’// GET /api/public/channels/{channelId}/messages
   794вҶ’getPublicMessages(channelId: string, query: PaginationQuery): Promise<PublicMessagesResponse>
   795вҶ’```
   796вҶ’
   797вҶ’### 9.2 Module M5: Business Logic

#### 6.3.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PRIVATE

When visibility changes to `PRIVATE`:
1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)

3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ invalidates guest view cache

---

### 6.4 Rationale

The LLM had to be reprompted here to clarify which protocols (RPC vs REST) would be used for communications. This was an issue across the this whole dev spec. It was determined REST protocols would be used for public APIs and RPC for internal communications. 

The LLM also had to be reprompted to finalize what the event bus would be. It chose Redis Pub/Sub to allow for visibility change updates to propagate. The cache keying also needed to be updated to match earlier updates. 

## 7. Development Risks and Failures

### 7.1 Runtime Failures

| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
| RF-1 | API Server crash | Toggle action fails | Auto-restart; client retries | Medium | Medium |
| RF-2 | Lost runtime state | Stale visibility displayed | Cache invalidation on recovery | Low | Low |
| RF-3 | Database corruption | Incorrect visibility; privacy breach | Restore from backup; audit reconciliation | Very Low | Critical |
| RF-4 | Invalid state transition | Channel may become public unintentionally | Server-side transition validation | Low | High |
| RF-5 | RPC failure | "Network error" shown | Retry with exponential backoff; circuit breaker | Medium | Medium |
| RF-6 | Server overload | Slow response or timeout | Rate limiting; horizontal scaling | Medium | Medium |
| RF-7 | Out of RAM | Server unresponsive | Memory limits; vertical scaling | Low | High |
| RF-8 | Database out of space | Write operations fail | Storage alerts; archive old audit logs | Low | High |

### 7.2 Connectivity Failures

| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
| CF-1 | Lost network | "Connection lost" banner | Auto-reconnect with backoff | Medium | Medium |
| CF-2 | Lost DB connection | API returns 503 | Connection pool health checks; failover | Low | High |
| CF-3 | Traffic spike | Increased latency | CDN caching; auto-scaling | Medium | Medium |
| CF-4 | Search engine API down | Indexing updates delayed | Queue failed notifications; retry | Medium | Low |

### 7.3 Hardware Failures

| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
| HF-1 | App server down | Service unavailable | Multi-AZ deployment; LB health checks | Low | High |
| HF-2 | Bad config loaded | Unpredictable behavior | Config validation on startup; rollback | Low | High |
| HF-3 | System relocation | Temporary outage | Blue-green deployment; DNS TTL management | Very Low | Medium |

### 7.4 Security Failures

| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
| IF-1 | DDoS attack | Service degradation | CloudFlare DDoS protection; rate limiting | Medium | High |
| IF-2 | OS compromise | Full system breach | Incident response; rebuild from clean images | Very Low | Critical |
| IF-3 | Code tampering | Malicious behavior | Code signing; integrity monitoring | Very Low | Critical |
| IF-4 | Database theft | Privacy breach | Encryption at rest; access logging | Low | Critical |
| IF-5 | Bot spam | Public channels flooded | CAPTCHA; rate limiting; content moderation | Medium | Medium |
| IF-6 | Session hijacking | Unauthorized changes | Secure cookies; session binding; anomaly detection | Low | High |

### 7.5 Failure Priority Matrix

```

### 7.6 Rationale

Minor reprompting was needed to standardize the rate-limiting policy. It did not affect this section, but other specs mentioned rate-limiting so it had to be added to this one as a failure. Otherwise the failure modes and resolutions make sense, and there are no obvious gaps in errors.

## 8. Technology Stack

| Label | Technology | Version | Purpose | Source |
|-------|------------|---------|---------|-------|
| T1 | TypeScript | 5.3+ | Primary language (client + server) | https://www.typescriptlang.org/ |
| T2 | React | 18.2+ | Frontend UI framework | https://react.dev/ |
| T3 | Next.js | 14.0+ | SSR/SSG framework (SEO-critical for public pages) | https://nextjs.org/ |
| T4 | Node.js | 20 LTS | Server runtime | https://nodejs.org/ |
| T5 | PostgreSQL | 16+ | Primary database (ACID, JSONB, enums) | https://www.postgresql.org/ |
| T6 | Redis | 7.2+ | Caching, session storage, EventBus (Pub/Sub) | https://redis.io/ |
| T7 | Prisma | 5.8+ | Type-safe ORM with migrations | https://www.prisma.io/ |
| T8 | tRPC | 10.45+ | End-to-end typesafe APIs (authenticated internal) | https://trpc.io/ |
| T9 | Zod | 3.22+ | Runtime schema validation (integrates with tRPC) | https://zod.dev/ |
| T10 | TailwindCSS | 3.4+ | Utility-first CSS framework | https://tailwindcss.com/ |
| T11 | CloudFlare | N/A | CDN and DDoS protection | https://www.cloudflare.com/ |
| T12 | Docker | 24+ | Containerization | https://www.docker.com/ |
| T13 | Google Search Console API | v1 | Programmatic indexing/de-indexing | https://developers.google.com/webmaster-tools |

| T15 | Jest | 29+ | Unit/integration testing | https://jestjs.io/ |
| T16 | Playwright | 1.40+ | Cross-browser E2E testing | https://playwright.dev/ |
| T17 | sanitize-html | 2.12+ | XSS prevention / HTML sanitization for public-facing content (Node.js-native) | https://github.com/apostrophecms/sanitize-html |

> **Convention:** tRPC is used for authenticated internal APIs between client and server. Public-facing endpoints (sitemaps, public channel pages, robots.txt) use REST for maximum compatibility with crawlers and third-party consumers.

### 8.1 EventBus

**Technology:** Redis Pub/Sub (T6)

Event types consumed/produced across specs:

| Event | Source Spec | Description |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | Channel Visibility Toggle (this spec) | Emitted when channel visibility state changes |
| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | New message in a public channel |
| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message edited in a public channel |
| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message deleted from a public channel |
| `META_TAGS_UPDATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Meta tags regenerated for a channel |

### 8.2 Rationale

Significant reprompting was necessary here because of conflicting tech stacks across each spec. This spec was missing DOMPurify, which would be needed to sanitize and generate sitemaps with other public content. The communication APIs being a mix of RPC and REST was also detected by the LLM here, requiring prompting to fix it. The LLM then determined to use RPC for authenticated internal APIs, while public endpoints would be REST for compatibility with web indexers.

Finally, the LLM made a shared tech-stack document that would be used across each spec.

#### 9.1.1 CL-C4.1 ChannelController

**Public Methods (Authenticated, tRPC):**

```typescript
// Get channel settings including visibility
getChannelSettings(
  channelId: string,          // UUID
  context: AuthenticatedContext
): Promise<ChannelSettingsResponse>

// Update channel visibility
updateChannelVisibility(
  channelId: string,
  body: VisibilityUpdateRequest,  // { visibility: ChannelVisibility }
  context: AuthenticatedContext
): Promise<VisibilityUpdateResponse>

// Get visibility change audit history
getVisibilityAuditLog(
  channelId: string,
  query: AuditLogQuery,       // { limit?, offset?, startDate? }
  context: AuthenticatedContext
): Promise<AuditLogResponse>
```

**Private Methods:**

```typescript
private validateAdminAccess(userId: string, channelId: string): Promise<boolean>
private mapToResponse(channel: Channel): ChannelSettingsResponse
```

#### 9.1.2 CL-C4.2 PublicAccessController

**Public Methods (Unauthenticated, REST):**

```typescript
// GET /c/{serverSlug}/{channelSlug}
getPublicChannel(

): Promise<PublicChannelPage>

// GET /sitemap/{serverSlug}.xml

// GET /robots.txt

// GET /api/public/channels/{channelId}/messages
getPublicMessages(channelId: string, query: PaginationQuery): Promise<PublicMessagesResponse>
```

erDiagram
    servers ||--o{ channels : "has"

### 4.2 Enum Definition

```sql
CREATE TYPE channel_visibility AS ENUM ('PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NO_INDEX', 'PRIVATE');
CREATE TYPE channel_type AS ENUM ('TEXT', 'VOICE', 'ANNOUNCEMENT');
CREATE TYPE user_status AS ENUM ('ONLINE', 'IDLE', 'DND', 'OFFLINE');
CREATE TYPE role_type AS ENUM ('OWNER', 'ADMIN', 'MODERATOR', 'MEMBER', 'GUEST');
```

### 4.3 Index Strategy (Canonical Set)

All indexes below are the **authoritative, merged** set across all three feature specs:

```sql
-- Channels
CREATE INDEX idx_channels_server_visibility ON channels(server_id, visibility);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_channels_server_slug ON channels(server_id, slug);
CREATE INDEX idx_channels_visibility_indexed ON channels(visibility, indexed_at)
  WHERE visibility = 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE';
CREATE INDEX idx_channels_visibility ON channels(visibility)
  WHERE visibility IN ('PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NO_INDEX');

-- Messages
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_channel_time ON messages(channel_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_channel_not_deleted ON messages(channel_id, created_at DESC)
  WHERE is_deleted = FALSE;

-- Visibility Audit Log
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_channel_time ON visibility_audit_log(channel_id, timestamp DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor ON visibility_audit_log(actor_id, timestamp DESC);

-- Servers
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_servers_slug ON servers(slug);
CREATE INDEX idx_servers_public ON servers(is_public) WHERE is_public = TRUE;

-- Generated Meta Tags
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_meta_tags_channel ON generated_meta_tags(channel_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_meta_tags_needs_regen ON generated_meta_tags(needs_regeneration)
  WHERE needs_regeneration = TRUE;
```

#### 9.2.1 CL-C5.1 ChannelVisibilityService

```typescript
// Set channel visibility with validation
setVisibility(
  channelId: string, newVisibility: ChannelVisibility,
  actorId: string, ipAddress: string
): Promise<VisibilityChangeResult>

getVisibility(channelId: string): Promise<ChannelVisibility>
canChangeVisibility(channelId: string, actorId: string): Promise<boolean>

private validateTransition(
  current: ChannelVisibility, next: ChannelVisibility
): ValidationResult

private emitVisibilityChange(event: VisibilityChangeEvent): Promise<void>
```

#### 9.2.2 CL-C5.2 IndexingService

```typescript
updateSitemap(serverId: string): Promise<void>
notifySearchEngines(url: string, action: 'INDEX' | 'REMOVE'): Promise<NotificationResult>
generateCanonicalUrl(serverId: string, channelId: string): string
getRobotsDirectives(visibility: ChannelVisibility): RobotsDirectives
```

#### 9.2.3 CL-C5.3 PermissionService

```typescript
canManageChannel(userId: string, channelId: string): Promise<boolean>
isServerAdmin(userId: string, serverId: string): Promise<boolean>
getEffectivePermissions(userId: string, channelId: string): Promise<PermissionSet>
```

#### 9.2.4 CL-C5.4 AuditLogService

```typescript
logVisibilityChange(entry: AuditLogEntry): Promise<void>
getAuditHistory(channelId: string, options: AuditQueryOptions): Promise<AuditLogEntry[]>
exportAuditLog(channelId: string, format: 'JSON' | 'CSV'): Promise<Buffer>
```

### 9.3 Module M6: Data Access

#### 9.3.1 CL-C6.1 ChannelRepository (Consolidated)

```typescript
findById(channelId: string): Promise<Channel | null>
findBySlug(serverSlug: string, channelSlug: string): Promise<Channel | null>
update(channelId: string, data: Partial<Channel>): Promise<Channel>
findPublicByServerId(serverId: string): Promise<Channel[]>
getVisibility(channelId: string): Promise<ChannelVisibility>
getMetadata(channelId: string): Promise<ChannelMetadata>

private invalidateCache(channelId: string): Promise<void>
private getCacheKey(channelId: string): string
```

### 9.4 Rate Limiting

| Consumer Type | Limit | Window | Scope |
|---------------|-------|--------|-------|
| Human users (authenticated) | 100 req | 1 min | Per user |
| Verified bots / crawlers | 1000 req | 1 min | Per bot identity |

Rate limits apply to all API endpoints. Exceeding limits returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header.

### 9.5 Rationale

The LLM had generated mismatched class methods and variables from before and now. It had to be reprompted to recouncile the differences and create missing functions both here and in previous sections. Like mentioned before, it also had to be reprompted to standardize a ratelimiting policy here instead of arbitrary values elsewhere.

The separation of public APIs, business logic, and data access layers is good practice in large systems, so I agree with the LLM's decisions here.

---

## 10. Public Interfaces

### 10.1 Cross-Module Interfaces

#### Client (M1вҖ“M3) вҶ’ API Gateway (M4):

| Method | Class | Used For |
|--------|-------|----------|
| getChannelSettings() | ChannelController | Loading channel settings |
| updateChannelVisibility() | ChannelController | Visibility toggle |
| getVisibilityAuditLog() | ChannelController | Audit history display |
| getPublicChannel() | PublicAccessController | Viewing public channel |
| getPublicMessages() | PublicAccessController | Paginating public messages |

#### API Gateway (M4) вҶ’ Business Logic (M5):

| Method | Class | Used For |
|--------|-------|----------|
| setVisibility() | ChannelVisibilityService | Processing visibility updates |
| getVisibility() | ChannelVisibilityService | Reading current visibility |
| canChangeVisibility() | ChannelVisibilityService | Permission checking |
| canManageChannel() | PermissionService | Authorization |
| generateCanonicalUrl() | IndexingService | SEO headers |
| getRobotsDirectives() | IndexingService | SEO headers |
| getAuditHistory() | AuditLogService | Audit log endpoint |

#### Business Logic (M5) вҶ’ Data Access (M6):

| Method | Class | Used For |
|--------|-------|----------|
| findById() | ChannelRepository | Loading channel entity |
| findBySlug() | ChannelRepository | Slug-based channel lookup |
| update() | ChannelRepository | Persisting visibility changes |
| findPublicByServerId() | ChannelRepository | Sitemap generation |
| getVisibility() | ChannelRepository | Fast visibility check |
| getMetadata() | ChannelRepository | Channel metadata retrieval |
| create() | AuditLogRepository | Writing audit entries |
| findByChannelId() | AuditLogRepository | Reading audit history |

### 10.2 REST API Interface

```yaml
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Harmony Channel Visibility API
  version: 1.0.0

paths:
  /api/channels/{channelId}/visibility:
    patch:
      summary: Update channel visibility
      security:
        - bearerAuth: []
      parameters:
        - name: channelId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            format: uuid
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/VisibilityUpdateRequest'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Visibility updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/VisibilityUpdateResponse'
        '401':
          description: Unauthorized
        '403':
          description: Forbidden
        '404':
          description: Channel not found
        '429':
          description: Rate limit exceeded

components:
  schemas:
    ChannelVisibility:
      type: string
      enum: [PUBLIC_INDEXABLE, PUBLIC_NO_INDEX, PRIVATE]

    VisibilityUpdateRequest:
      type: object
      required: [visibility]
      properties:
        visibility:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelVisibility'

    VisibilityUpdateResponse:
      type: object
      properties:
        success:
          type: boolean
        channel:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelDTO'
        previousVisibility:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/ChannelVisibility'
        indexingStatus:
          type: string
          enum: [PENDING, INDEXED, NOT_INDEXED, REMOVAL_REQUESTED]
```

### 10.3 Cross-Spec Event Integration

When `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` is emitted:

| New Visibility | Downstream Action (SEO Spec) | Downstream Action (Guest View Spec) |
|---------------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Generate meta tags for channel | Warm guest view cache |
| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Update meta tags (add noindex) | Keep guest view cache (public content) |
| `PRIVATE` | Delete meta tags for channel | Invalidate guest view cache |

### 10.4 Rationale

The LLM correctly generated the public API specification. It did not need to be reprompted for any fixes here. The generated API specification is correct and exposes the endpoints necessary for this specific user story.

900вҶ’|-------------|-----------|-----|--------------|---------------------| 901вҶ’| `channel:{channelId}:visibility` | String (enum) | 3600s | M-B3 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event | 902вҶ’| `server:{serverId}:public_channels` | JSON (channel ID array) | 300s | M-B4 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event | 903вҶ’| `channel:msgs:{channelId}:page:{pageNum}` | JSON (PublicMessageDTO[]) | 60s | M-B4 | `MESSAGE_CREATED/EDITED/DELETED` | 904вҶ’| `server:{serverId}:info` | JSON (PublicServerDTO) | 300s | M-B4 | Server update | 905вҶ’| `guest:session:{sessionId}` | JSON (preferences) | 86400s | M-B2 | Session expiry | 906вҶ’| `meta:channel:{channelId}` | JSON (MetaTagSet) | 3600s | M-B5 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_*` | 907вҶ’| `analysis:channel:{channelId}` | JSON (ContentAnalysis) | 1800s | M-B5 | `MESSAGE_*` events | 908вҶ’ 909вҶ’### 4.5 Event Bus (Redis Pub/Sub) 910вҶ’ 911вҶ’```mermaid 912вҶ’graph LR 913вҶ’ subgraph Producers 914вҶ’ CVS["ChannelVisibilityService
(M-B3)"] 915вҶ’ MsgSvc["Message System
(future)"] 916вҶ’ end 917вҶ’ 918вҶ’ subgraph EventBus["Redis Pub/Sub"] 919вҶ’ VC["VISIBILITY_CHANGED"] 920вҶ’ MC["MESSAGE_CREATED"] 921вҶ’ ME["MESSAGE_EDITED"] 922вҶ’ MD["MESSAGE_DELETED"] 923вҶ’ MTU["META_TAGS_UPDATED"] 924вҶ’ end 925вҶ’ 926вҶ’ subgraph Consumers 927вҶ’ IdxSvc["IndexingService
(M-B6)"] 928вҶ’ MetaSvc["MetaTagService
(M-B5)"] 929вҶ’ CacheMgr["Cache Invalidator
(M-D3)"] 930вҶ’ BgWorker["Event-Driven Workers
(M-B7)"] 931вҶ’ SSEBridge["SSE Bridge
(events.router)"] 932вҶ’ end 933вҶ’ 934вҶ’ CVS --> VC 935вҶ’ MsgSvc --> MC 936вҶ’ MsgSvc --> ME 937вҶ’ MsgSvc --> MD 938вҶ’ MetaSvc --> MTU 939вҶ’ 940вҶ’ VC --> IdxSvc 941вҶ’ VC --> MetaSvc 942вҶ’ VC --> CacheMgr 943вҶ’ MC --> MetaSvc 944вҶ’ MC --> CacheMgr 945вҶ’ MC --> SSEBridge 946вҶ’ ME --> MetaSvc 947вҶ’ ME --> CacheMgr 948вҶ’ ME --> SSEBridge 949вҶ’ MD --> MetaSvc 950вҶ’ MD --> CacheMgr 951вҶ’ MD --> SSEBridge 952вҶ’ MTU --> BgWorker 953вҶ’``` 954вҶ’ 955вҶ’| Event | Payload | Producer | Consumers | 956вҶ’|-------|---------|----------|-----------| 957вҶ’| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | `{ channelId, serverId, oldVisibility, newVisibility, actorId, timestamp }` | ChannelVisibilityService (M-B3) | IndexingService (M-B6), MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3) | 958вҶ’| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | `{ messageId, channelId, authorId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | 959вҶ’| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | `{ messageId, channelId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | 960вҶ’| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | `{ messageId, channelId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | 961вҶ’| `META_TAGS_UPDATED` | `{ channelId, version, timestamp }` | MetaTagService (M-B5) | Background Workers (M-B7) for sitemap update | 962вҶ’ 963вҶ’### 4.6 Real-Time Client Transport (SSE) 964вҶ’ 965вҶ’The EventBus delivers events between server-side services, but clients require a persistent outbound channel to receive those events in real time. Harmony uses **Server-Sent Events (SSE)** вҖ” a lightweight, browser-native, HTTP/1.1-compatible push mechanism. 966вҶ’ 967вҶ’#### Why SSE over WebSockets 968вҶ’ 969вҶ’| Criterion | SSE | WebSockets | 970вҶ’|-----------|-----|------------| 971вҶ’| Direction | Server вҶ’ Client only | Bidirectional | 972вҶ’| Protocol | HTTP/1.1 (no upgrade) | WS upgrade required | 973вҶ’| Client library | `EventSource` (built-in) | `ws` / `socket.io` | 974вҶ’| Proxy / CDN compatibility | вң… Works everywhere | вҡ пёҸ Requires proxy support | 975вҶ’| Reconnection | Automatic (browser) | Manual | 976вҶ’| Fit for Harmony | вң… Clients only receive; mutations go via tRPC | вҖ” | 977вҶ’ 978вҶ’Harmony clients already send all mutations (send message, edit, delete) via tRPC. The SSE channel is receive-only, making WebSockets unnecessary complexity. 979вҶ’ 980вҶ’#### Endpoint 981вҶ’ 982вҶ’``` 983вҶ’GET /api/events/channel/:channelId?token= 984вҶ’``` 985вҶ’ 986вҶ’**Auth:** The browser `EventSource` API cannot set custom headers, so the JWT access token is passed as a `?token=` query parameter. The endpoint verifies it identically to `requireAuth`. 987вҶ’ 988вҶ’**Authorisation:** The handler checks `serverMember` membership before opening the stream, preventing access to PRIVATE channel events by non-members. 989вҶ’ 990вҶ’**SSE frame format:** 991вҶ’``` 992вҶ’event: message:created 993вҶ’data: {"id":"...","channelId":"...","author":{...},"content":"...","timestamp":"..."} 994вҶ’ 995вҶ’event: message:edited 996вҶ’data: {"id":"...","content":"...","editedAt":"..."} 997вҶ’ 998вҶ’event: message:deleted 999вҶ’data: {"messageId":"..."} 1000вҶ’``` 1001вҶ’ 1002вҶ’A heartbeat comment (`:\n\n`) is sent every 30 seconds to keep the connection alive through proxies and load balancers. 1003вҶ’ 1004вҶ’#### Event Flow (Message Created) 1005вҶ’ 1006вҶ’``` 1007вҶ’Browser Client A Express SSE Redis Pub/Sub Browser Client B 1008вҶ’ | | | | 1009вҶ’ |в”Җв”Җ tRPC sendMessage в”Җв”Җв–¶| | | 1010вҶ’ | |в”Җв”Җ INSERT message в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶DB | 1011вҶ’ | |в”Җв”Җ PUBLISH MESSAGE_CREATED в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶ | 1012вҶ’ | | |в”Җв”Җ notify subscriber в”Җв–¶| 1013вҶ’ | | | (SSE bridge) | 1014вҶ’ | |в—Җв”Җв”Җ response в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ| | 1015вҶ’ |в—Җв”Җв”Җ msg returned в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ| |в”Җв”Җ write SSE frame в”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶| 1016вҶ’ | (optimistic update) | | | вҶҗ message appears 1017вҶ’``` 1018вҶ’ 1019вҶ’#### Client Integration 1020вҶ’ 1021вҶ’The `useChannelEvents` React hook (`src/hooks/useChannelEvents.ts`) wraps `EventSource`: 1022вҶ’ 1023вҶ’```ts 1024вҶ’useChannelEvents({ 1025вҶ’ channelId, 1026вҶ’ onMessageCreated: (msg) => setMessages(prev => [...prev, msg]), 1027вҶ’ onMessageEdited: (msg) => setMessages(prev => prev.map(m => m.id === msg.id ? msg : m)), 1028вҶ’ onMessageDeleted: (id) => setMessages(prev => prev.filter(m => m.id !== id)), 1029вҶ’ enabled: isAuthenticated, 1030вҶ’}); 1031вҶ’``` 1032вҶ’ 1033вҶ’The hook deduplicates `message:created` events against messages already added optimistically by the sender's own tRPC callback, preventing double-rendering when the SSE event races the HTTP response. 1034вҶ’ 1035вҶ’--- 1036вҶ’ 1037вҶ’## 5. |-------------|-----------|-----|--------------|---------------------| | `channel:{channelId}:visibility` | String (enum) | 3600s | M-B3 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event | | `server:{serverId}:public_channels` | JSON (channel ID array) | 300s | M-B4 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event | | `channel:msgs:{channelId}:page:{pageNum}` | JSON (PublicMessageDTO[]) | 60s | M-B4 | `MESSAGE_CREATED/EDITED/DELETED` | | `server:{serverId}:info` | JSON (PublicServerDTO) | 300s | M-B4 | Server update | | `guest:session:{sessionId}` | JSON (preferences) | 86400s | M-B2 | Session expiry | | `meta:channel:{channelId}` | JSON (MetaTagSet) | 3600s | M-B5 | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_*` | | `analysis:channel:{channelId}` | JSON (ContentAnalysis) | 1800s | M-B5 | `MESSAGE_*` events | ### 4.5 Event Bus (Redis Pub/Sub) ```mermaid graph LR subgraph Producers CVS["ChannelVisibilityService
(M-B3)"] MsgSvc["Message System
(future)"] end subgraph EventBus["Redis Pub/Sub"] VC["VISIBILITY_CHANGED"] MC["MESSAGE_CREATED"] ME["MESSAGE_EDITED"] MD["MESSAGE_DELETED"] MTU["META_TAGS_UPDATED"] end subgraph Consumers IdxSvc["IndexingService
(M-B6)"] MetaSvc["MetaTagService
(M-B5)"] CacheMgr["Cache Invalidator
(M-D3)"] BgWorker["Event-Driven Workers
(M-B7)"] SSEBridge["SSE Bridge
(events.router)"] end CVS --> VC MsgSvc --> MC MsgSvc --> ME MsgSvc --> MD MetaSvc --> MTU VC --> IdxSvc VC --> MetaSvc VC --> CacheMgr MC --> MetaSvc MC --> CacheMgr MC --> SSEBridge ME --> MetaSvc ME --> CacheMgr ME --> SSEBridge MD --> MetaSvc MD --> CacheMgr MD --> SSEBridge MTU --> BgWorker ``` | Event | Payload | Producer | Consumers | |-------|---------|----------|-----------| | `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | `{ channelId, serverId, oldVisibility, newVisibility, actorId, timestamp }` | ChannelVisibilityService (M-B3) | IndexingService (M-B6), MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3) | | `MESSAGE_CREATED` | `{ messageId, channelId, authorId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | | `MESSAGE_EDITED` | `{ messageId, channelId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | | `MESSAGE_DELETED` | `{ messageId, channelId, timestamp }` | Message System | MetaTagService (M-B5), Cache Invalidator (M-D3), SSE Bridge | | `META_TAGS_UPDATED` | `{ channelId, version, timestamp }` | MetaTagService (M-B5) | Background Workers (M-B7) for sitemap update | ### 4.6 Real-Time Client Transport (SSE) The EventBus delivers events between server-side services, but clients require a persistent outbound channel to receive those events in real time. Harmony uses **Server-Sent Events (SSE)** вҖ” a lightweight, browser-native, HTTP/1.1-compatible push mechanism. #### Why SSE over WebSockets | Criterion | SSE | WebSockets | |-----------|-----|------------| | Direction | Server вҶ’ Client only | Bidirectional | | Protocol | HTTP/1.1 (no upgrade) | WS upgrade required | | Client library | `EventSource` (built-in) | `ws` / `socket.io` | | Proxy / CDN compatibility | вң… Works everywhere | вҡ пёҸ Requires proxy support | | Reconnection | Automatic (browser) | Manual | | Fit for Harmony | вң… Clients only receive; mutations go via tRPC | вҖ” | Harmony clients already send all mutations (send message, edit, delete) via tRPC. The SSE channel is receive-only, making WebSockets unnecessary complexity. #### Endpoint ``` GET /api/events/channel/:channelId?token= ``` **Auth:** The browser `EventSource` API cannot set custom headers, so the JWT access token is passed as a `?token=` query parameter. The endpoint verifies it identically to `requireAuth`. **Authorisation:** The handler checks `serverMember` membership before opening the stream, preventing access to PRIVATE channel events by non-members. **SSE frame format:** ``` event: message:created data: {"id":"...","channelId":"...","author":{...},"content":"...","timestamp":"..."} event: message:edited data: {"id":"...","content":"...","editedAt":"..."} event: message:deleted data: {"messageId":"..."} ``` A heartbeat comment (`:\n\n`) is sent every 30 seconds to keep the connection alive through proxies and load balancers. #### Event Flow (Message Created) ``` Browser Client A Express SSE Redis Pub/Sub Browser Client B | | | | |в”Җв”Җ tRPC sendMessage в”Җв”Җв–¶| | | | |в”Җв”Җ INSERT message в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶DB | | |в”Җв”Җ PUBLISH MESSAGE_CREATED в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶ | | | |в”Җв”Җ notify subscriber в”Җв–¶| | | | (SSE bridge) | | |в—Җв”Җв”Җ response в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ| | |в—Җв”Җв”Җ msg returned в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ| |в”Җв”Җ write SSE frame в”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶| | (optimistic update) | | | вҶҗ message appears ``` #### Client Integration The `useChannelEvents` React hook (`src/hooks/useChannelEvents.ts`) wraps `EventSource`: ```ts useChannelEvents({ channelId, onMessageCreated: (msg) => setMessages(prev => [...prev, msg]), onMessageEdited: (msg) => setMessages(prev => prev.map(m => m.id === msg.id ? msg : m)), onMessageDeleted: (id) => setMessages(prev => prev.filter(m => m.id !== id)), enabled: isAuthenticated, }); ``` The hook deduplicates `message:created` events against messages already added optimistically by the sender's own tRPC callback, preventing double-rendering when the SSE event races the HTTP response. --- ## 5. Unified API Surface ### 5.1 Authenticated APIs (tRPC) All tRPC procedures are mounted under `/trpc` and require a valid session. #### tRPC Context Every tRPC procedure receives a typed `TRPCContext` injected by `createContext` in `src/trpc/init.ts`: ```typescript // src/trpc/init.ts export interface TRPCContext {

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Guest Public Channel View
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Anonymous Access to Public Channel Content
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Guest User (Searcher), I want to view the full contents of a public channel via a direct URL without being prompted to log in, so that I can get the answer to my specific question immediately without the friction of creating an account I might only use once.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B2** (Access Control), **M-B4** (Content Delivery), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

# Development Specification: Guest Public Channel View

## Feature: Anonymous Access to Public Channel Content

**User Story:** As a Guest User (Searcher), I want to view the full contents of a public channel via a direct URL without being prompted to log in, so that I can get the answer to my specific question immediately without the friction of creating an account I might only use once.

> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B2** (Access Control), **M-B4** (Content Delivery), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

---

## 1. Header

### Version and Date

| Version | Date       | Description                              |
|---------|------------|------------------------------------------|
| 1.0     | 2026-02-12 | Initial development specification        |
| 2.0     | 2026-02-15 | Cross-spec consolidation: label fixes, cache key alignment, convention standardization |

### Author and Role

| Author        | Role                    | Version |
|---------------|-------------------------|---------|
| Claude (AI)   | Specification Author    | 1.0, 2.0 |
| dblanc        | Project Lead            | 1.0     |
| Aiden-Barrera | Project Member          | 2.0     |

---

   349вҶ’### 2.4 Rationale
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’The archtecture diagram is justified because client server split abstracts from the guest the authorization logic the server handles and caching requests significantly helps with performance for storing the same content that will be served to many users. Furthermore, the importance of authorization lies in the fact whether a channel is public or not, to make sure guests can't see private channels.

### 2.4 Rationale

The archtecture diagram is justified because client server split abstracts from the guest the authorization logic the server handles and caching requests significantly helps with performance for storing the same content that will be served to many users. Furthermore, the importance of authorization lies in the fact whether a channel is public or not, to make sure guests can't see private channels. 

---

    в”Ӯ + memberCount: number   в”Ӯ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    в”Ӯ CL-D11 Attachment       в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ <<Entity>>              в”Ӯ
    в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
    в”Ӯ + id: UUID              в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ + messageId: UUID       в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ + filename: string      в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ + url: string           в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ + contentType: string   в”Ӯ
    в”Ӯ + sizeBytes: number     в”Ӯ
    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### 3.1 Rationale

The class diagram clearly separates the entities that will be needed for displaying the public channel to the guest user, specifically with only grabbing public entities such as the server, messages and owner of the message to avoid exposing private channel information. 

---

## 4. List of Classes

### 4.1 Edge Layer (W1)

| Label | Class Name | Type | Purpose |
|-------|------------|------|---------|
| CL-W1.1 | CacheRouter | Edge Worker | Routes requests through CDN cache, determines cache hit/miss, manages cache keys |

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Channel Visibility Toggle
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Public/Indexable Channel Control
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Community Administrator, I want to toggle specific channels as "Public/Indexable" or "Private," so that I can control which parts of my community are exposed to the open web while keeping sensitive social conversations private.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B3** (Visibility Management), **M-B6** (SEO & Indexing, shared), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’---
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’## 1. Header
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’### 1.1 Version and Date
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’| Version | Date       | Description                              |
    16вҶ’|---------|------------|------------------------------------------|
    17вҶ’| 1.0     | 2026-02-12 | Initial development specification        |
    18вҶ’| 2.0     | 2026-02-12 | Cross-spec consolidation and fixes       |
    19вҶ’
    20вҶ’### 1.2 Author and Role
    21вҶ’
    22вҶ’| Author        | Role                    | Version |
    23вҶ’|---------------|-------------------------|---------|
    24вҶ’| Claude (AI)   | Specification Author    | 2.0     |
    25вҶ’| dblanc        | Project Lead            | 1.0     |
    26вҶ’| AvanishKulkarni | Project Lead | 2.0 |
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’### 1.3 Rationale
    29вҶ’Header with versioning and authors.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’---
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’## 2. Architecture Diagram
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’### 2.1 System Overview
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’```
    38вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    39вҶ’в”Ӯ                         CLIENT LAYER                            в”Ӯ
    40вҶ’в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ

   160вҶ’### 2.3 Rationale
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’This follows a clear model-view-controller architecture, where the client can view channels and control their visibility state with the M1 Admin Dashboard controller. The underlying model is represented by the server layer, which handles get/set operations and any necessary side-effects for search engine indexing. 
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’The underlying data layer uses a short-term caching layer to reduce database accesses and syncs with external systems. 
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’We had to prompt edits to this to ensure the database columns were not mismatched across each architecture diagram. The Redis cache key pattern was also inconsistent across specs, so we had to prompt fixes for that as well.
   167вҶ’

   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’> **Sitemap Ownership:** `IndexingService` (CL6.1 / C5.2) is the canonical owner of sitemap generation and search engine notification across all Harmony specs. Other features (e.g., SEO Meta Tag Generation) emit events that this service consumes to trigger sitemap updates.
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’### 3.1 Rationale
   275вҶ’
   276вҶ’After having an LLM review this spec, the canonical owner of the sitemap generation should be the IndexingService. There was a discrepancy between this spec and the seo-meta-tag-generation spec on what would consume server updates and generate new sitemaps for external services. 
   277вҶ’
   278вҶ’Significant inconsistencies existed between section 2, 3, 9, and 10, so a verification pass was necessary after the document was generated to fix them. 
   279вҶ’

   343вҶ’### 4.7 Rationale
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’Like the previous section, I had to reprompt to fix inconsistencies. The LLM also noticed that the ChannelRepository interface/class has discrepancies across each spec, so it consolidated each of them together. The class diagrams correctly display the interactions between each class, so no update was needed there.

   446вҶ’### 5.4 Rationale
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’The first diagram correctly tracks the state changes for all possible channel states. No changes or reprompting the LLM was necessary for this section. The channel will be public, public & indexable, or private. A simplification of the roles-based access control in Discord, but covers the general idea. 
   449вҶ’
   450вҶ’The second diagram correctly tracks the state transitions for the channel visibility permission. It is quite simple so the model did not need to the reprompted for any changes.
   451вҶ’

   525вҶ’#### 6.1.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PUBLIC_INDEXABLE
   526вҶ’
   527вҶ’When visibility changes to `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`:
   528вҶ’1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
   529вҶ’2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event вҶ’ generates meta tags for the channel
   530вҶ’3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ warms guest view cache

   615вҶ’#### 6.3.1 Cross-Spec Integration: Visibility вҶ’ PRIVATE
   616вҶ’
   617вҶ’When visibility changes to `PRIVATE`:
   618вҶ’1. Emit `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event via EventBus (Redis Pub/Sub)
   619вҶ’2. **SEO Meta Tag Generation spec** consumes event вҶ’ deletes meta tags for the channel
   620вҶ’3. **Guest Public Channel View spec** consumes event вҶ’ invalidates guest view cache

   623вҶ’
   624вҶ’### 6.4 Rationale
   625вҶ’
   626вҶ’The LLM had to be reprompted here to clarify which protocols (RPC vs REST) would be used for communications. This was an issue across the this whole dev spec. It was determined REST protocols would be used for public APIs and RPC for internal communications. 
   627вҶ’
   628вҶ’The LLM also had to be reprompted to finalize what the event bus would be. It chose Redis Pub/Sub to allow for visibility change updates to propagate. The cache keying also needed to be updated to match earlier updates. 
   629вҶ’
   630вҶ’## 7. Development Risks and Failures
   631вҶ’
   632вҶ’### 7.1 Runtime Failures
   633вҶ’
   634вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   635вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   636вҶ’| RF-1 | API Server crash | Toggle action fails | Auto-restart; client retries | Medium | Medium |
   637вҶ’| RF-2 | Lost runtime state | Stale visibility displayed | Cache invalidation on recovery | Low | Low |
   638вҶ’| RF-3 | Database corruption | Incorrect visibility; privacy breach | Restore from backup; audit reconciliation | Very Low | Critical |
   639вҶ’| RF-4 | Invalid state transition | Channel may become public unintentionally | Server-side transition validation | Low | High |
   640вҶ’| RF-5 | RPC failure | "Network error" shown | Retry with exponential backoff; circuit breaker | Medium | Medium |
   641вҶ’| RF-6 | Server overload | Slow response or timeout | Rate limiting; horizontal scaling | Medium | Medium |
   642вҶ’| RF-7 | Out of RAM | Server unresponsive | Memory limits; vertical scaling | Low | High |
   643вҶ’| RF-8 | Database out of space | Write operations fail | Storage alerts; archive old audit logs | Low | High |
   644вҶ’
   645вҶ’### 7.2 Connectivity Failures
   646вҶ’
   647вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
   648вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   649вҶ’| CF-1 | Lost network | "Connection lost" banner | Auto-reconnect with backoff | Medium | Medium |
   650вҶ’| CF-2 | Lost DB connection | API returns 503 | Connection pool health checks; failover | Low | High |
   651вҶ’| CF-3 | Traffic spike | Increased latency | CDN caching; auto-scaling | Medium | Medium |
   652вҶ’| CF-4 | Search engine API down | Indexing updates delayed | Queue failed notifications; retry | Medium | Low |
   653вҶ’
   654вҶ’### 7.3 Hardware Failures

   658вҶ’| HF-1 | App server down | Service unavailable | Multi-AZ deployment; LB health checks | Low | High |
   659вҶ’| HF-2 | Bad config loaded | Unpredictable behavior | Config validation on startup; rollback | Low | High |
   660вҶ’| HF-3 | System relocation | Temporary outage | Blue-green deployment; DNS TTL management | Very Low | Medium |

   663вҶ’

   665вҶ’|-------|-------------|--------------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
   666вҶ’| IF-1 | DDoS attack | Service degradation | CloudFlare DDoS protection; rate limiting | Medium | High |
   667вҶ’| IF-2 | OS compromise | Full system breach | Incident response; rebuild from clean images | Very Low | Critical |
   668вҶ’| IF-3 | Code tampering | Malicious behavior | Code signing; integrity monitoring | Very Low | Critical |
   669вҶ’| IF-4 | Database theft | Privacy breach | Encryption at rest; access logging | Low | Critical |
   670вҶ’| IF-5 | Bot spam | Public channels flooded | CAPTCHA; rate limiting; content moderation | Medium | Medium |
   671вҶ’| IF-6 | Session hijacking | Unauthorized changes | Secure cookies; session binding; anomaly detection | Low | High |

   688вҶ’### 7.6 Rationale
   689вҶ’
   690вҶ’Minor reprompting was needed to standardize the rate-limiting policy. It did not affect this section, but other specs mentioned rate-limiting so it had to be added to this one as a failure. Otherwise the failure modes and resolutions make sense, and there are no obvious gaps in errors.

   716вҶ’> **Convention:** tRPC is used for authenticated internal APIs between client and server. Public-facing endpoints (sitemaps, public channel pages, robots.txt) use REST for maximum compatibility with crawlers and third-party consumers.
   717вҶ’
   718вҶ’### 8.1 EventBus
   719вҶ’
   720вҶ’**Technology:** Redis Pub/Sub (T6)
   721вҶ’
   722вҶ’Event types consumed/produced across specs:
   723вҶ’
   724вҶ’| Event | Source Spec | Description |
   725вҶ’|-------|-------------|-------------|
   726вҶ’| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | Channel Visibility Toggle (this spec) | Emitted when channel visibility state changes |
   727вҶ’| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | New message in a public channel |
   728вҶ’| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message edited in a public channel |
   729вҶ’| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message deleted from a public channel |
   730вҶ’| `META_TAGS_UPDATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Meta tags regenerated for a channel |
   731вҶ’
   732вҶ’### 8.2 Rationale
   733вҶ’
   734вҶ’Significant reprompting was necessary here because of conflicting tech stacks across each spec. This spec was missing DOMPurify, which would be needed to sanitize and generate sitemaps with other public content. The communication APIs being a mix of RPC and REST was also detected by the LLM here, requiring prompting to fix it. The LLM then determined to use RPC for authenticated internal APIs, while public endpoints would be REST for compatibility with web indexers.
   735вҶ’
   736вҶ’Finally, the LLM made a shared tech-stack document that would be used across each spec.

   859вҶ’### 9.4 Rate Limiting
   860вҶ’
   861вҶ’| Consumer Type | Limit | Window | Scope |
   862вҶ’|---------------|-------|--------|-------|

   864вҶ’| Verified bots / crawlers | 1000 req | 1 min | Per bot identity |

   866вҶ’Rate limits apply to all API endpoints. Exceeding limits returns `429 Too Many Requests` with `Retry-After` header.

   868вҶ’### 9.5 Rationale
   869вҶ’
   870вҶ’The LLM had generated mismatched class methods and variables from before and now. It had to be reprompted to recouncile the differences and create missing functions both here and in previous sections. Like mentioned before, it also had to be reprompted to standardize a ratelimiting policy here instead of arbitrary values elsewhere.
   871вҶ’
   872вҶ’The separation of public APIs, business logic, and data access layers is good practice in large systems, so I agree with the LLM's decisions here.

   985вҶ’### 10.3 Cross-Spec Event Integration
   986вҶ’
   987вҶ’When `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` is emitted:
   988вҶ’
   989вҶ’| New Visibility | Downstream Action (SEO Spec) | Downstream Action (Guest View Spec) |
   990вҶ’|---------------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------|
   991вҶ’| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Generate meta tags for channel | Warm guest view cache |
   992вҶ’| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Update meta tags (add noindex) | Keep guest view cache (public content) |
   993вҶ’| `PRIVATE` | Delete meta tags for channel | Invalidate guest view cache |
   994вҶ’
   995вҶ’### 10.4 Rationale
   996вҶ’
   997вҶ’The LLM correctly generated the public API specification. It did not need to be reprompted for any fixes here. The generated API specification is correct and exposes the endpoints necessary for this specific user story. 
   998вҶ’
   999вҶ’Significant inconsistencies in classes existed between sections 2, 3, 9, and 10, so a verification pass was necessary after the document was generated to fix them.

  1007вҶ’#### D7.1 channels
  1008вҶ’
  1009вҶ’**Runtime Class:** CL-D1 Channel
  1010вҶ’
  1011вҶ’| Column | Database Type | Constraints | Description |
  1012вҶ’|--------|--------------|-------------|-------------|
  1013вҶ’| id | UUID | PRIMARY KEY | Unique channel identifier |
  1014вҶ’| server_id | UUID | FOREIGN KEY вҶ’ servers(id), NOT NULL, INDEX | Parent server reference |
  1015вҶ’| name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Display name |
  1016вҶ’| slug | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL, UNIQUE per server | URL-safe identifier |

  1018вҶ’| topic | TEXT | NULL | Channel topic/description |

  1020вҶ’| indexed_at | TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE | NULL | When channel was added to sitemap |

  1024вҶ’**Enum Definition:**
  1025вҶ’```sql
  1026вҶ’CREATE TYPE visibility_enum AS ENUM ('PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NO_INDEX', 'PRIVATE');
  1027вҶ’```
  1028вҶ’
  1029вҶ’**Indexes (Canonical Set вҖ” merged from all specs):**
  1030вҶ’```sql
  1031вҶ’-- Composite index for server-scoped visibility queries
  1032вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_channels_server_visibility ON channels(server_id, visibility);
  1033вҶ’
  1034вҶ’-- Unique slug per server
  1035вҶ’CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_channels_server_slug ON channels(server_id, slug);
  1036вҶ’
  1037вҶ’-- Partial index for indexable channels (sitemap generation)
  1038вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_channels_visibility_indexed ON channels(visibility, indexed_at)
  1039вҶ’  WHERE visibility = 'PUBLIC_INDEXABLE';
  1040вҶ’
  1041вҶ’-- Partial index for all public channels (guest view queries)
  1042вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_channels_visibility ON channels(visibility)
  1043вҶ’  WHERE visibility IN ('PUBLIC_INDEXABLE', 'PUBLIC_NO_INDEX');
  1044вҶ’```

1вҶ’# Development Specification: Guest Public Channel View
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’## Feature: Anonymous Access to Public Channel Content
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’**User Story:** As a Guest User (Searcher), I want to view the full contents of a public channel via a direct URL without being prompted to log in, so that I can get the answer to my specific question immediately without the friction of creating an account I might only use once.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md). The mapping from this spec's class labels to the unified module labels is in В§10 of that document. Key modules contributed by this feature: **M-B2** (Access Control), **M-B4** (Content Delivery), **M-D1** (Data Access, shared).

   349вҶ’### 2.4 Rationale
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’The archtecture diagram is justified because client server split abstracts from the guest the authorization logic the server handles and caching requests significantly helps with performance for storing the same content that will be served to many users. Furthermore, the importance of authorization lies in the fact whether a channel is public or not, to make sure guests can't see private channels.

   537вҶ’### 3.1 Rationale
   538вҶ’
   539вҶ’The class diagram clearly separates the entities that will be needed for displaying the public channel to the guest user, specifically with only grabbing public entities such as the server, messages and owner of the message to avoid exposing private channel information.

   650вҶ’### 4.10 Rationale
   651вҶ’
   652вҶ’The list of classes clearly states the moving parts for ensuring guest user can access public channels and their messages, with handling caching. The classes cover all the responsibilities needed for this feature to function from route handling to retrieving the public data to formatting the response to the guest. The inclusion of caching and bot detection justified since retrieval of the same content from multiple guest is unnecessary more work on the server.

   656вҶ’## 5. State Diagrams
   657вҶ’
   658вҶ’### 5.1 System State Variables
   659вҶ’
   660вҶ’| Variable | Type | Description |
   661вҶ’|----------|------|-------------|
   662вҶ’| request.path | string | Current URL path being requested |
   663вҶ’| channel.visibility | ChannelVisibility | Visibility state of requested channel |
   664вҶ’| cache.status | CacheStatus | Whether response is cached (HIT/MISS/STALE) |
   665вҶ’| guest.sessionId | string | Anonymous session identifier |
   666вҶ’| page.loadState | LoadState | Current page loading state |
   667вҶ’| messages.pagination | PaginationState | Current pagination position |
   668вҶ’
   669вҶ’> **Convention:** `is_public` (boolean) applies to **servers** вҖ” whether the server appears in discovery. `visibility` (enum: `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE`, `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX`, `PRIVATE`) applies to **channels** вҖ” whether channel content is accessible to guests and/or indexed by search engines.

   855вҶ’
   856вҶ’### 5.5 Rationale
   857вҶ’
   858вҶ’These states were chosen to show the phases a guest can be for viewing a public channel, the states handle critical edge cases a guest can experience since the endpoints are publicily accessible such as trying to visit a private channel or channel that isn't cached. The state also has no login redirect due to the fact that this feature is supposed allow anonymous users to access public channels. Importantly each state has a clear end to each phase so the guest ins't stuck in a loop state that they can't get out off.
   859вҶ’
   860вҶ’---
   861вҶ’
   862вҶ’## 6. Flow Charts
   863вҶ’
   864вҶ’### 6.1 Scenario: Guest Views Public Channel from Search Result
   865вҶ’
   866вҶ’**Scenario Description:** A guest user clicks a search result link that leads to a public channel. The system serves the full content without any login prompts, allowing the user to immediately access the information they were searching for.

  1293вҶ’### 6.5 Cross-Spec Integration: VISIBILITY_CHANGED Event Consumption
  1294вҶ’
  1295вҶ’When the Channel Visibility Toggle spec emits a `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` event (via Redis Pub/Sub EventBus), the Guest Public Channel View system reacts as follows:
  1296вҶ’
  1297вҶ’| New Visibility | Guest View Action |
  1298вҶ’|---------------|-------------------|
  1299вҶ’| `PUBLIC_INDEXABLE` | Warm guest view cache for channel; begin serving public content |
  1300вҶ’| `PUBLIC_NO_INDEX` | Keep guest view cache (content still public); update `X-Robots-Tag` to `noindex` |
  1301вҶ’| `PRIVATE` | Invalidate all guest view caches for channel; return 403/404 on subsequent requests |
  1302вҶ’
  1303вҶ’**Event Payload Consumed:**
  1304вҶ’```typescript
  1305вҶ’interface VisibilityChangeEvent {
  1306вҶ’  channelId: string;        // UUID
  1307вҶ’  oldVisibility: ChannelVisibility;
  1308вҶ’  newVisibility: ChannelVisibility;
  1309вҶ’  actorId: string;          // UUID of admin who made the change
  1310вҶ’  timestamp: DateTime;
  1311вҶ’}
  1312вҶ’```
  1313вҶ’
  1314вҶ’**Cache Keys Invalidated on PRIVATE:**
  1315вҶ’- `channel:{channelId}:visibility`
  1316вҶ’- `channel:{channelId}:msgs:*` (all pages)
  1317вҶ’- `server:{serverId}:info`
  1318вҶ’
  1319вҶ’### 6.6 Rationale 
  1320вҶ’
  1321вҶ’The flow charts depict the major flow cases a guest will experience for Harmony. The first flow covers the primary case that the guest visits the public channel from search engine result, which is the primary aim for Harmony, to be able to provide public channel information to guests without the need of logging in. The second flow covers the edge case a guests tries to visit a private channel, safely redirecting them without revealing any sensitive information about the server. The third flow covers the guest loading more messages of the channel, allowing the guest to infinitely scroll older messages. The fourth flow covers the public channels to be crawlable by search engine bots, so guests searching for information related to public channels can see it. 
  1322вҶ’
  1323вҶ’---
  1324вҶ’
  1325вҶ’## 7. Development Risks and Failures
  1326вҶ’
  1327вҶ’### 7.1 Runtime Failures
  1328вҶ’
  1329вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1330вҶ’|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1331вҶ’| RF-1 | SSR render crash | 500 error page | React hydration failure | Error boundary catches; shows fallback UI | Medium | High |
  1332вҶ’| RF-2 | Database query timeout | Slow page load or timeout | Connection pool exhaustion | Query optimization; read replicas; timeout handling | Medium | Medium |
  1333вҶ’| RF-3 | Cache corruption | Stale or incorrect content shown | Cache-DB inconsistency | Cache invalidation; serve from DB | Low | Medium |
  1334вҶ’| RF-4 | Memory leak in SSR | Gradual performance degradation | Node.js process OOM | Process recycling; memory monitoring | Low | High |
  1335вҶ’| RF-5 | Infinite scroll breaks | Users can't load more messages | Client JS error | Fallback pagination links; error logging | Medium | Low |
  1336вҶ’| RF-6 | SEO data generation fails | Missing meta tags | Empty title/description | Default fallback values; monitoring | Low | Medium |
  1337вҶ’| RF-7 | Content filter regex timeout | Slow response on large messages | CPU spike | Regex timeout limits; message size limits | Low | Medium |
  1338вҶ’
  1339вҶ’### 7.2 Connectivity Failures
  1340вҶ’
  1341вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1342вҶ’|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1343вҶ’| CF-1 | CDN edge outage | Regional unavailability | Cache layer bypassed | Multi-CDN failover; origin direct access | Low | High |
  1344вҶ’| CF-2 | Origin unreachable | 502/503 errors | CDN can't reach server | Health checks; auto-scaling; stale-while-revalidate | Low | High |
  1345вҶ’| CF-3 | Database connection loss | 500 errors | All queries fail | Connection retry; replica failover | Low | Critical |
  1346вҶ’| CF-4 | Redis cache unavailable | Slower responses | Cache misses; DB load increase | Degrade gracefully; serve from DB | Medium | Medium |
  1347вҶ’| CF-5 | Slow network to guest | Long load times | Time to first byte high | CDN edge caching; image optimization | Medium | Medium |
  1348вҶ’
  1349вҶ’### 7.3 Hardware Failures
  1350вҶ’
  1351вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1352вҶ’|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1353вҶ’| HF-1 | Web server crash | Brief unavailability | Container restart | Auto-restart; load balancer rerouting | Low | Medium |
  1354вҶ’| HF-2 | Database server down | Complete outage | All writes/reads fail | Automatic failover to replica | Very Low | Critical |
  1355вҶ’| HF-3 | Storage full | New messages not visible | Write failures | Storage alerts; auto-scaling storage | Low | High |
  1356вҶ’
  1357вҶ’### 7.4 Security/Intruder Failures
  1358вҶ’
  1359вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1360вҶ’|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1361вҶ’| IF-1 | DDoS on public pages | Service degradation | Resource exhaustion | CloudFlare DDoS protection; rate limiting | Medium | High |
  1362вҶ’| IF-2 | Scraping abuse | None (internal cost) | Bandwidth/compute abuse | Bot detection; rate limiting; CAPTCHA | High | Medium |
  1363вҶ’| IF-3 | Visibility bypass attempt | 403 error (if working) | Security log entry | Input validation; server-side checks | Medium | Critical |
  1364вҶ’| IF-4 | XSS via message content | Malicious script execution | User session compromise | Content sanitization; CSP headers | Low | Critical |
  1365вҶ’| IF-5 | Cache poisoning | Wrong content served | Cache serves malicious response | Cache key validation; purge capability | Very Low | Critical |
  1366вҶ’| IF-6 | Enumeration attack | None (if protected) | Load on database | Rate limiting; no existence disclosure | Medium | Low |
  1367вҶ’
  1368вҶ’### 7.5 Content/Data Failures
  1369вҶ’
  1370вҶ’| Label | Failure Mode | User-Visible Effect | Internal Effect | Recovery Procedure | Likelihood | Impact |
  1371вҶ’|-------|--------------|--------------------|-----------------|--------------------|------------|--------|
  1372вҶ’| DF-1 | Private content exposed | Privacy breach | Visibility check bypassed | Audit; immediate visibility fix; notification | Very Low | Critical |
  1373вҶ’| DF-2 | User ID leaked in public DTO | Privacy concern | PII exposure | DTO review; immediate patch | Low | High |
  1374вҶ’| DF-3 | Deleted message shown | Confusing content | Soft delete not respected | Query filter fix; cache purge | Low | Medium |
  1375вҶ’| DF-4 | Attachment not loading | Broken images/files | Storage access issue | CDN retry; fallback placeholder | Medium | Low |
  1376вҶ’

  1391вҶ’  Very Low  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ CF-1,CF-2 в”Ӯ HF-2,IF-5    в”Ӯ

  1393вҶ’```
  1394вҶ’
  1395вҶ’### 7.7 Rationale 
  1396вҶ’
  1397вҶ’The development risks and failures categories were chosen to represent the primary threat this feature can face. The runtime and connectivity failures are catagorized due to the feature being a publicly accessible endpoint that any guest can use, leading to unpredictable traffic volumes. Intruder risks face the highest priorty due to the endpoint having no authentication since guests aren't required to log in.

  1412вҶ’| T8 | tRPC | 10.45+ | End-to-end typesafe APIs (authenticated internal) | Type-safe client-server communication | https://trpc.io/ |

  1427вҶ’> **Convention:** tRPC is used for authenticated internal APIs between client and server. Public-facing endpoints (public channel pages, sitemaps, robots.txt) use REST for maximum compatibility with crawlers and third-party consumers.
  1428вҶ’
  1429вҶ’### 8.1 EventBus
  1430вҶ’
  1431вҶ’**Technology:** Redis Pub/Sub (T6)
  1432вҶ’
  1433вҶ’Event types consumed by this spec:
  1434вҶ’
  1435вҶ’| Event | Source Spec | Description |
  1436вҶ’|-------|-------------|-------------|
  1437вҶ’| `VISIBILITY_CHANGED` | Channel Visibility Toggle | Channel visibility state changed; invalidate/warm caches |
  1438вҶ’| `MESSAGE_CREATED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | New message in public channel; invalidate message cache |
  1439вҶ’| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message edited; invalidate affected cache pages |
  1440вҶ’| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | SEO Meta Tag Generation | Message deleted; invalidate affected cache pages |
  1441вҶ’
  1442вҶ’### 8.2 Rationale
  1443вҶ’
  1444вҶ’The technology stack was chosen to align with Harmony's architecture design and meet the needs of this feature. The primary langauge to be used for Harmony is Typescript, the reason is ensuring type safety accross the website, reducing runtime errors. Redis will serve our caching layer, for fast reads and session storage. Next.js was selected for its out of the box tools and capabilities it provide for Harmony such as SSR.

  1818вҶ’### 9.5 Rate Limiting
  1819вҶ’
  1820вҶ’| Consumer Type | Limit | Window | Enforcement |
  1821вҶ’|--------------|-------|--------|-------------|
  1822вҶ’| Human (anonymous) | 100 requests | 1 minute | Token bucket per IP |
  1823вҶ’| Verified bot (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.) | 1000 requests | 1 minute | User-Agent verification |
  1824вҶ’| Suspicious pattern | CAPTCHA challenge | After 500 page views/hour | Behavioral analysis |
  1825вҶ’
  1826вҶ’### 9.6 Pagination Precedence
  1827вҶ’
  1828вҶ’When both cursor and page parameters are provided, cursor-based pagination takes precedence:
  1829вҶ’
  1830вҶ’1. If `before` or `after` (cursor) is provided, use cursor-based pagination (ignore `page`)
  1831вҶ’2. Otherwise, fall back to offset-based pagination using `page` and `limit`
  1832вҶ’3. Default: `page=1`, `limit=50`
  1833вҶ’
  1834вҶ’### Rationale
  1835вҶ’
  1836вҶ’The APIs for Guest Public Channel View, clearly handle the usage of the feature for being able to publicily access channels that are from the original search message. The first section APIs' purpose is for handling showing the information for the channel in which was derived from the search query and being able to load further messages in the channel. Next section APIs' purpose is for handling the server information once there, such as showing the server information and other public channels inside that server.
  1837вҶ’
